message.content:
You are implementing one Job Jockey pace (₢BHAAL, "lode-podvm-platform-fanout") in the Recipe Bottle codebase at /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster. An opus architect has already settled all the design; your job is faithful implementation against a blessed schema. Read the files named below before editing — ground every change in the actual code, follow the surrounding idiom exactly (naming, comment density, error handling).

HARD CONSTRAINTS (violating any is a failure):
- Do NOT git commit, git add, git reset/restore/checkout/stash/clean — none. The operator commits.
- Do NOT run any live Google Cloud Build or any fixture/tabtarget that submits to GCB (no `tt/rbw-tf`, no service/crucible suites, no `tt/rbw-cC`). The opus parent runs the live gate later.
- Never use `cd`. Use absolute paths. Never pipe a tabtarget through tee/tail/head/grep/2>&1 — run it directly, then read `../logs-buk/last.txt`.
- Lint bash ONLY via `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` (never raw shellcheck). Build theurge via `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh`. Run the fast suite via `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` (no GCB dependency). These three are your self-check; run them at the end and report results from `../logs-buk/last.txt`.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ORIENTATION — read these first
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
- Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh        — the podvm capture body (the verb rbld_immure; helpers zrbld_immure_*). Family dispatch already landed.
- Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl07-immure-select.py — the cloud select step (authors the vouch envelope + the slot/result format). The design-bearing file.
- Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh, rbgjl03-conclave-capture.sh, rbgjl04-underpin-capture.sh — the other three envelope+slot authors.
- Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh — pushes :rbi_vouch as an OPAQUE blob. It must NOT read envelope keys. Verify it stays opaque; do not convert it.
- Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh       — podvm constants (RBGC_LODE_PODVM_*_SELECTION etc.), around lines 216-314.
- Tools/rbk/rbldl_Lifecycle.sh      — rbld_augur (reads/decodes the envelope via jq paths) and rbld_divine.
- Tools/rbk/rbldr_Reliquary.sh, Tools/rbk/rbldb_Bole.sh — body extracts that parse the slot format (`.slot_1.stamp`). Find every `.slot_1` reader.
- Tools/rbk/rblds_Spine.sh          — zrbld_spine_extract is SLOT-AGNOSTIC (base64-decodes raw buildStepOutputs[N]); it does NOT parse slot keys, so it needs NO change.
- Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md — read "The fourth clause — wire formats are named homes" (~line 134) and the move-catalog entry around lines 330-342 (ACGm_108; "First worked application: the Lode vouch envelope (rblv_...)").
- Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/CBG-CloudBuildGuide.md — substitution/buildStepOutputs discipline (CBh_103, the 4KB slot ceiling).
- Memos/memo-20260608-lode-podvm-cerebro-experiment.md — §5 has the native machine-os curated leaf set; §3.x has the index/descriptor structure.
- Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSLI-lode_immure.adoc and RBSLA-lode_augur.adoc — spec prose naming envelope fields (linked-term .adoc; preserve MCM formatting and one-sentence-per-line where present).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TASK 1 — Sprue the vouch envelope to rblv_, schema "rbld-vouch-2"
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Operator-cinched: every JSON wire format this project authors on both sides carries a minted key-sprue. The vouch envelope's sprue is `rblv_`. NO dual-dialect, NO back-compat (pre-MVP, depot disposable): every author writes rblv_ keys, augur reads rblv_ ONLY, schema value becomes "rbld-vouch-2".

Blessed v2 envelope shape (compact JSON; this is the exact key set):
{
  "rblv_schema":        "rbld-vouch-2",
  "rblv_kind":          "<brand, e.g. podvm-native>",
  "rblv_lode":          "<stamp>",
  "rblv_acquired_at":   "<envelope authored-at, ISO8601 Z>",
  "rblv_acquired_by":   "<mason SA email>",
  "rblv_capture_build": "<BUILD_ID of this authoring>",
  "rblv_trust_grade":   "<recorded|verified>",
  "rblv_signature":     null,
  "rblv_members": [
    {
      "rblv_name":          "<member tag>",
      "rblv_origin":        "<source coord>",
      "rblv_digest":        "<sha256:...>",
      "rblv_verification":  "<recorded|verified>",
      "rblv_tags":          ["<tag>", ...],
      "rblv_acquired_at":   "<this leaf's capture time>",     // PODVM-ONLY (see TASK 4)
      "rblv_capture_build": "<build id that captured this leaf>"  // PODVM-ONLY
    }
  ]
}

Convert all FOUR envelope authors (rbgjl07 python; rbgjl01/03/04 bash) to emit rblv_ keys and "rbld-vouch-2". Convert augur's jq in rbldl_Lifecycle.sh to read rblv_ paths (`.rblv_trust_grade`, `.rblv_kind`, `.rblv_lode`, `.rblv_acquired_at`, `.rblv_acquired_by`, `.rblv_capture_build`, `.rblv_signature`, `.rblv_members[]...`). Keep augur's DISPLAYED LABELS unchanged ("Trust grade:", "Kind:", etc.) — only the jq key paths change. Envelope-level rblv_acquired_at / rblv_capture_build mean "when this envelope was authored" uniformly for all kinds. For single-capture kinds (bole/reliquary/wsl) the per-member rblv_acquired_at/rblv_capture_build are OMITTED (authoring == capture). Only podvm carries per-member times.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TASK 2 — Sprue the slot/result format to rbls_ (NEW sprue, blessed)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The buildStepOutputs payload `{"slot_1":{"stamp":...,"vouch":...}}` is a DISTINCT wire format from the envelope, so per ACG's fourth clause it gets its OWN sprue: `rbls_` (Lode Slot, terminal child of rbl). New keys:
{ "rbls_slot_1": { "rbls_stamp": "<stamp>", "rbls_vouch": { ...the rblv_ envelope... } } }
Convert the four authors' RESULT= construction (rbgjl01/03/04 bash `RESULT="{\"slot_1\":...}"`; rbgjl07 python `result = {"slot_1": {...}}`) and every body extract that reads `.slot_1.stamp` (rbldr_Reliquary, rbldv_Immure, rbldb_Bole, and any other — grep `slot_1` across Tools/rbk to be exhaustive). zrbld_spine_extract is slot-agnostic — leave it.
In ACG, record rbls_ as ACGm_108's SECOND worked application (the move-catalog entry that currently names rblv_ as the first). One line, matching the existing phrasing.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TASK 3 — Native full-curation constant
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
In rbgc_Constants.sh, replace the modest 2-leaf RBGC_LODE_PODVM_NATIVE_SELECTION ("qemu:x86_64 wsl:x86_64") with the full curated native set from memo-20260608 §5 (the machine-os family's curated {disktype:arch} leaves — read the memo for the exact list; it is the ~8-leaf set). Format is space-separated `disktype:arch` rows in the alt arch spelling (x86_64/aarch64). Update the surrounding comment block: this pace now proves native full curation; drop the "FOLLOWING pace" deferral language for native curation since it's landing here.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TASK 4 — Refresh mode (Architecture H: host computes present-set, passes as ONE substitution)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
WHY refresh exists: podvm is recorded-grade against a hostile rotating upstream (quay deletes within days). Refresh = "expand the captured leaf set of an EXISTING Lode at the SAME version" without re-pulling/re-resolving already-held leaves (re-resolving could silently repoint a member tag to rebuilt bytes, or fail if upstream aged out). It is ADD-ONLY / PRESERVE-ORIGINALS.

INTERFACE:
- Fresh:   `rbw-lI <family> <version>`         → mints a new stamp `<kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>`, present-set empty, version from the arg.
- Refresh: `rbw-lI <family> --refresh <touchmark>` → reuses <touchmark> as the stamp; NO version argument (structurally cannot bump); the locked version is DERIVED from the target Lode's existing envelope (parse it from any member's rblv_origin, format "<family>:<version>").
Parse this in rbld_immure (Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh). BUZ_FOLIO is the family; $1 is either the version (fresh) or the literal `--refresh` followed by the touchmark in $2 (refresh). Update buc_doc_param accordingly.

SOURCE OF TRUTH = the GAR package's MEMBER TAGS, never the envelope (the envelope pushes last in rbgjl02, so a crash between member-cp and vouch-push leaves new tags present but un-enveloped). On refresh, compute the present-set HOST-SIDE (reuse the augur read path — list tags + read the existing :rbi_vouch envelope; for createTime use the GAR REST machinery in rbuh/rbgv that augur-adjacent code already uses). Classify each member-tag the widened selection would produce:
  • PRESENT + ENVELOPED (tag in GAR ∧ member in existing envelope) → preserve that member entry VERBATIM (keeps its true per-member rblv_acquired_at/rblv_capture_build). Never re-resolve upstream.
  • PRESENT + ORPHAN (tag in GAR ∧ absent from envelope) → recover honestly: digest from the tag's manifest, rblv_acquired_at = the GAR tag/version createTime, rblv_verification = "recorded". No upstream touch.
  • ABSENT (in widened selection, no GAR tag) → resolve from upstream in the cloud select step, cp, per-member time = NOW / this BUILD_ID.
Pass the preserved+orphan member objects to the select step as ONE NEW substitution (e.g. _RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED), a COMPACT JSON array string; default "[]" for fresh capture (fresh is the empty-present-set case of the same path — keep one code path). Worst case 8 members ≈ 2.7KB < the 4000-byte conservative substitution guard (rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh:438 cites the 4096 hard limit) — add a comment noting Arch H holds through ~11 members and a future widening past that must fall to in-pool recovery.

SELECT STEP (rbgjl07-immure-select.py) merge logic:
  - Read the new _RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED env var (JSON array; default []). Index it by member tag.
  - For each leaf in _RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION: if its member_tag is in the preserved index → splice the preserved member object straight into the envelope's rblv_members; do NOT fetch from quay; do NOT add it to /workspace/immure_selection.txt (the cp/residency contract).
  - Else (absent) → resolve from the quay index exactly as today, append to immure_selection.txt, and author its member with per-member rblv_acquired_at = the acquired_at timestamp and rblv_capture_build = os.environ["BUILD_ID"].
  - The final envelope's rblv_members = preserved ∪ orphan ∪ newly-resolved. The cp/residency selection list = newly-resolved (absent) only. Envelope-level rblv_acquired_at/rblv_capture_build = NOW / this build (the authoring event).
  - Keep the existing "die loud listing available (arch,disktype) on no-match" behavior for absent leaves.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TASK 5 — Legend / asymmetry note (carry the architect's exact rationale)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
At the envelope's spec definition site (RBSLI-lode_immure.adoc, which owns the immure envelope; add a short pointer comment at the rbgjl07 author too), document TWO things in prose:
  (a) Per-member rblv_acquired_at/rblv_capture_build are PODVM-ONLY because only podvm refreshes — i.e. only podvm accumulates members across multiple authoring events. The asymmetry TRACKS refresh-capability; a future uniformity sweep must NOT "fix" bole/wsl to carry per-member times.
  (b) The dual-position rblv_acquired_at is SELF-DISCLOSING: in a refreshed envelope the member-level times sit EARLIER than the envelope-level time, so the artifact itself displays the authored-vs-captured distinction.
Update RBSLI's field list and RBSLA's augur-output description to the rblv_ names and the refresh mode. Preserve .adoc/MCM formatting.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TASK 6 — Fixture (three tiers, NO new test seams)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
In Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs (the podvm-lifecycle fixture rbtdrc_podvm_lifecycle and its manifest in rbtdrm_manifest.rs):
  • RECURRING SERVICE: extend podvm-lifecycle to prove fan-out + REFRESH on the WSL family (its production curation is already lean — use the real RBGC_LODE_PODVM_WSL_SELECTION, NO fixture-special selection). Add a refresh case: capture a subset, refresh to widen, assert the widened member tags are present AND the originals' tags are unchanged (same digest). Use the existing colophons (immure with --refresh, augur, divine, jettison, banish). Existing envelope assertions ride augur's RENDERED TEXT (labels unchanged) so they are insulated from the key rename — confirm that, don't rewrite them.
  • FAST TIER: add a pure host-side bash case asserting zrbld_immure_resolve_family resolves BOTH brands (podvm-wsl → vw + machine-os-wsl family; podvm-native → vn + machine-os family). No GCB. This is the free regression cover for the native arm. Wire it into the fast suite per the existing fast-fixture pattern.
  • ONE-TIME GATE: do NOT add the full-8-leaf native capture to any suite. The opus parent runs it live once at verification and records it in the wrap. Leave a comment in the fixture noting native full curation is gated one-time, not recurring.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SELF-CHECK (run at the very end, report each from ../logs-buk/last.txt)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh`        — must be clean.
2. `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh`             — theurge must build (the .rs fixture changes compile). Note: the build regenerates rbtdgc_consts.rs and claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md from the zipper — if those show modified, that is expected/yours.
3. `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`    — fast suite (incl. your new resolve_family case) must pass.
For the python step, also run `python3 -m py_compile Tools/rbk/rbgjl/rbgjl07-immure-select.py`.

DELIVERABLE: a final report listing (1) every file changed and what changed, (2) the exact native curation set you took from memo §5, (3) the refresh interface + the present-set substitution name you used, (4) self-check results (shellcheck count, build status, fast-suite pass count), (5) anything you were unsure about or deviated on. Do not commit. Do not run anything that hits GCB.

- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - podvm body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   immure — wall in the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay family into
21	#            a Lode (Director credentials)
22	# The podvm kind rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
23	# kind-specific data — the immure recipe (anon-quay index select + gcrane cp-by-digest
24	# + blob-residency guard + vouch-push), the substitutions blob, and the touchmark-fact
25	# extract — and composes them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No
26	# build-submission or step-composition machinery lives here.
27	#
28	# Shape: opaque-blob (like wsl/underpin) x multi-member (like reliquary/conclave). Each
29	# selected disk leaf is a single-platform OCI artifact (empty config + one zstd blob);
30	# the cohort of leaves rides as N member tags within one GAR package. One verb spans
31	# BOTH quay families (podvm-wsl: quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl, podvm-native:
32	# quay.io/podman/machine-os) via the family argument — not two verbs.
33	#
34	# Recorded-at-acquisition grade: quay rotates podvm out within days and publishes no
35	# durable checksum, so RB attests only the leaf digest captured (trust-on-first-
36	# acquisition). Cloud-side only; the workstation assembles no bytes, only the
37	# declarative family + version + curated leaf-set.
38	
39	set -euo pipefail
40	
41	# Immure is capture-pure: it writes no consumer config. It hands the captured
42	# touchmark forward through two bare single-form chaining facts
43	# (RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK + RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND/the podvm brand). The provenance
44	# envelope lives only in GAR (:rbi_vouch tag, pushed cloud-side by rbgjl02), never
45	# host-side. Consumption (a host's `podman machine init` from the captured seed) is a
46	# separate, deferred layer that reads these facts — not part of immure.
47	
48	######################################################################
49	# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)
50	
51	# Internal: resolve the family argument to its (kind-letter, quay-family, selection,
52	# brand) tuple. The brand IS the operator-typed argument and the envelope kind field.
53	# podvm-wsl is fixture-proven this pace; podvm-native is wired but its full curation +
54	# refresh mode are the FOLLOWING pace (see rbgc_Constants podvm selection block).
55	# Args: family   Sets: z_kind, z_quay_family, z_selection (caller-scoped locals)
56	zrbld_immure_resolve_family() {
57	  zrbld_sentinel
58	  local -r zz_family="${1:?Family required}"
59	  case "${zz_family}" in
60	    "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL}")
61	      z_kind="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_WSL}"
62	      z_quay_family="${RBGC_LODE_PODVM_FAMILY_WSL}"
63	      z_selection="${RBGC_LODE_PODVM_WSL_SELECTION}"
64	      ;;
65	    "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE}")
66	      z_kind="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_NATIVE}"
67	      z_quay_family="${RBGC_LODE_PODVM_FAMILY_NATIVE}"
68	      z_selection="${RBGC_LODE_PODVM_NATIVE_SELECTION}"
69	      ;;
70	    *)
71	      buc_die "Unknown podvm family '${zz_family}' (expected ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE})"
72	      ;;
73	  esac
74	}
75	
76	# Internal: compose the immure capture recipe (anon-quay index select + gcrane
77	# cp-by-digest + blob-residency guard + vouch-push) and its substitutions blob, then
78	# ride the capture spine to submit and poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build
79	# knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool, regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe,
80	# the substitutions, and the inscribe-grade poll ceiling (the multi-GB leaf copies
81	# want the larger ceiling). Four steps across three builders: index-select rides the
82	# gcloud builder (python3 — parses the upstream OCI index, which the no-jq bash GCB
83	# discipline does not cover; rbgjl06 precedent); gcrane cp and the vouch-push ride the
84	# floating gcrane builder (busybox); the residency HEAD rides the Debian docker builder
85	# (curl, allowlisted). The recipe-row ORDER is part of the contract: vouch (rbgjl02)
86	# runs strictly after residency (rbgjl09) — the vouch artifact never precedes the
87	# anti-hollow-mirror guard. podvm is vessel-less (no reliquary slot), so its gcrane
88	# rides the floating bootstrap builder, same tier as conclave/wsl — pinning defers to
89	# the bootstrap-builder digest-pin itch (RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary).
90	# Args: token brand quay_family version selection stamp
91	zrbld_immure_submit() {
92	  zrbld_sentinel
93	
94	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
95	  local -r z_brand="${2:?Brand required}"
96	  local -r z_quay_family="${3:?Family required}"
97	  local -r z_version="${4:?Version required}"
98	  local -r z_selection="${5:?Selection required}"
99	  local -r z_stamp="${6:?Stamp required}"
100	
101	  buc_step "Constructing immure capture recipe"
102	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
103	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
104	
105	  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the spine.
106	  # Select on the gcloud builder (python3 — index parse); residency on the Debian docker
107	  # builder (curl HEAD); cp + vouch on the floating gcrane builder (busybox). The gcrane
108	  # builder reads public quay anonymously and pushes GAR ambiently (google.Keychain ->
109	  # Mason SA).
110	  local -r z_recipe=(
111	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl07-immure-select.py|${ZRBLD_GCLOUD_BUILDER}|immure-select|python3"
112	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl08-immure-capture.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|immure-capture|busybox"
113	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl09-immure-residency.sh|${ZRBLD_GOOGLE_DOCKER_BUILDER}|immure-residency|bash"
114	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|assemble-push-vouch|busybox"
115	  )
116	
117	  buc_log_args "Composing immure substitutions blob"
118	  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}subs.json"
119	  jq -n \
120	    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
121	    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
122	    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
123	    --arg zjq_tag_sprue    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
124	    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
125	    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED}" \
126	    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
127	    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
128	    --arg zjq_stamp        "${z_stamp}" \
129	    --arg zjq_brand        "${z_brand}" \
130	    --arg zjq_family       "${z_quay_family}" \
131	    --arg zjq_version      "${z_version}" \
132	    --arg zjq_selection    "${z_selection}" \
133	    '{
134	      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:        $zjq_gar_host,
135	      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:        $zjq_gar_path,
136	      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:      $zjq_lodes_root,
137	      _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE:       $zjq_tag_sprue,
138	      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:       $zjq_tag_vouch,
139	      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:     $zjq_trust_grade,
140	      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:    $zjq_vouch_schema,
141	      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:     $zjq_acquired_by,
142	      _RBGL_LODE_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp,
143	      _RBGL_PODVM_BRAND:     $zjq_brand,
144	      _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY:    $zjq_family,
145	      _RBGL_PODVM_VERSION:   $zjq_version,
146	      _RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION: $zjq_selection
147	    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
148	    || buc_die "Failed to compose immure substitutions blob"
149	
150	  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
151	    "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "Immure" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_INSCRIBE}" \
152	    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}" \
153	    "${z_recipe[@]}"
154	}
155	
156	# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed immure build and emit
157	# the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand enum). The
158	# select step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the host-minted stamp in
159	# slot_1; the capture/residency/vouch steps write no output. Immure captures exactly
160	# one Lode (the cohort is one package), so exactly one slot is populated. The
161	# provenance envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR (rbgjl02 pushed it
162	# under :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the touchmark a consumer needs.
163	# Args: brand
164	zrbld_immure_extract() {
165	  zrbld_sentinel
166	  local -r z_brand="${1:?Brand required}"
167	
168	  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"
169	
170	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}output.json"
171	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
172	
173	  buc_log_args "Immure output:"
174	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"
175	
176	  local -r z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}stamp.txt"
177	  jq -r '.slot_1.stamp // empty' "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
178	    || buc_die "Failed to read podvm stamp from immure output"
179	  local -r z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
180	  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Immure output carried no stamp in slot_1"
181	
182	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
183	    || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
184	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${z_brand}" \
185	    || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
186	  buc_success "Immure captured Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${z_brand})"
187	}
188	
189	######################################################################
190	# External Functions (rbld_*)
191	
192	rbld_immure() {
193	  zrbld_sentinel
194	
195	  buc_doc_brief "Wall in the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay family into a Lode (podvm kind, rbi_ld capture)"
196	  buc_doc_param "family"  "Quay family — ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE}"
197	  buc_doc_param "version" "Podman version tag on the family index (e.g. 5.6)"
198	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
199	
200	  # Two declarative arguments (no FQIN — see RBSLI): the param1 channel routes the
201	  # first to BUZ_FOLIO and forwards the rest, so family is the folio and version the
202	  # first positional. The curated leaf-set is a per-family constant; the cloud step
203	  # resolves leaf digests from the live index at capture time.
204	  local -r z_brand="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
205	  local -r z_version="${1:-}"
206	  test -n "${z_brand}"   || buc_die "family argument required (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE})"
207	  test -n "${z_version}" || buc_die "version argument required (e.g. 5.6)"
208	
209	  local z_kind="" z_quay_family="" z_selection=""
210	  zrbld_immure_resolve_family "${z_brand}"
211	  buc_info "Immure family: ${z_brand} (${z_quay_family}:${z_version}), leaves: ${z_selection}"
212	
213	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
214	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
215	
216	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
217	  local z_token=""
218	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
219	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
220	
221	  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns the
222	  # stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file. podvm
223	  # kind-letters are two characters (vw/vn); the augur touchmark regex accepts the
224	  # multi-letter prefix.
225	  local -r z_stamp="${z_kind}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
226	
227	  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
228	
229	  zrbld_immure_submit "${z_token}" "${z_brand}" "${z_quay_family}" "${z_version}" "${z_selection}" "${z_stamp}"
230	  zrbld_immure_extract "${z_brand}"
231	
232	  buc_success "Immure complete: ${z_quay_family}:${z_version} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
233	}
234	
235	# eof

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	# RBGJL Step 07: Select podvm disk leaves from a quay family index (the immure select)
3	# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest (floating bootstrap; python3 + urllib +
4	#   json — the reliquary-less capture rides this, not a pinned tool). Python, not bash:
5	#   this step PARSES a structured upstream OCI index (correlate platform.architecture +
6	#   annotations.disktype within a child descriptor, extract a third field), which the
7	#   "no jq, author-by-hand with grep+cut" bash GCB discipline does not cover. python's
8	#   stdlib json/urllib are the native tools — the rbgjl06-package-delete.py precedent,
9	#   and the .py file is outside the bash command-allowlist (CBG polyglot, CBp_ rules).
10	# Substitutions (automapSubstitutions provides as env vars):
11	#   _RBGL_GAR_HOST _RBGL_GAR_PATH _RBGL_LODES_ROOT _RBGL_LODE_STAMP _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE
12	#   _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
13	#   _RBGL_PODVM_BRAND _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY _RBGL_PODVM_VERSION _RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION
14	#
15	# podvm is recorded-at-acquisition: quay rotates these images out within days and
16	# publishes no durable checksum, so RB attests only the leaf digest observed at
17	# capture. There is no checksum to verify (unlike wsl); the integrity guard is the
18	# downstream blob-residency HEAD (rbgjl09) plus gcrane cp's digest-faithful copy.
19	#
20	# Selection list staged at /workspace/immure_selection.txt — one row per selected
21	# leaf, the contract for rbgjl08 (capture) and rbgjl09 (residency):
22	#   <member_tag>|<leaf_manifest_digest>|<layer_blob_digest>|<layer_blob_size>
23	# No field can carry a '|' (tags, sha256: digests, decimal sizes), so busybox
24	# IFS='|' read splits it cleanly. The envelope (/workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json)
25	# and the stamp roster (/workspace/lode_stamps.txt) are the contract for rbgjl02.
26	
27	import datetime
28	import json
29	import os
30	import re
31	import sys
32	import urllib.error
33	import urllib.parse
34	import urllib.request
35	
36	ACCEPT_INDEX = (
37	    "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,"
38	    "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json"
39	)
40	ACCEPT_MANIFEST = (
41	    "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json,"
42	    "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"
43	)
44	
45	
46	def die(msg):
47	    print(f"FATAL: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
48	    sys.exit(1)
49	
50	
51	def require_env(name):
52	    val = os.environ.get(name, "")
53	    if not val:
54	        die(f"{name} missing")
55	    return val
56	
57	
58	def http_get(url, headers):
59	    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)
60	    with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
61	        return resp.read()
62	
63	
64	def get_pull_token(registry, repo):
65	    # Registry-v2 anon Bearer: probe /v2/, read the 401 Www-Authenticate challenge
66	    # for the realm + service, then fetch a pull-scoped token. quay's anonymous
67	    # rate-limiting is the failure mode — fail loud, never retry-loop.
68	    probe = f"https://{registry}/v2/"
69	    try:
70	        http_get(probe, {})
71	        return None  # registry needs no auth
72	    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
73	        if e.code != 401:
74	            die(f"unexpected {e.code} probing {probe}: {e.reason}")
75	        challenge = e.headers.get("Www-Authenticate", "")
76	    realm = re.search(r'realm="([^"]*)"', challenge)
77	    service = re.search(r'service="([^"]*)"', challenge)
78	    if not realm:
79	        die(f"no Bearer realm in Www-Authenticate from {registry}: {challenge!r}")
80	    params = {"service": (service.group(1) if service else registry),
81	              "scope": f"repository:{repo}:pull"}
82	    token_url = realm.group(1) + "?" + urllib.parse.urlencode(params)
83	    try:
84	        body = http_get(token_url, {})
85	    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
86	        die(f"anon token fetch failed ({e.code}) at {token_url}: {e.reason}")
87	    data = json.loads(body)
88	    token = data.get("token") or data.get("access_token")
89	    if not token:
90	        die("no token in anon auth response")
91	    return token
92	
93	
94	def fetch_manifest(registry, repo, ref, token, accept):
95	    url = f"https://{registry}/v2/{repo}/manifests/{ref}"
96	    headers = {"Accept": accept}
97	    if token:
98	        headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {token}"
99	    try:
100	        return json.loads(http_get(url, headers))
101	    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
102	        die(f"failed to fetch manifest {ref} ({e.code}): {e.reason}")
103	
104	
105	def main():
106	    gar_host = require_env("_RBGL_GAR_HOST")
107	    gar_path = require_env("_RBGL_GAR_PATH")
108	    lodes_root = require_env("_RBGL_LODES_ROOT")
109	    stamp = require_env("_RBGL_LODE_STAMP")
110	    sprue = require_env("_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE")
111	    brand = require_env("_RBGL_PODVM_BRAND")
112	    family = require_env("_RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY")
113	    version = require_env("_RBGL_PODVM_VERSION")
114	    selection = require_env("_RBGL_PODVM_SELECTION")
115	
116	    print("=== Select podvm disk leaves from quay family index ===")
117	
118	    # Decompose the family into the registry-v2 host + repository (quay.io/podman/...).
119	    if "/" not in family:
120	        die(f"family '{family}' has no registry/repo split")
121	    registry, repo = family.split("/", 1)
122	    print(f"Registry: {registry}  Repo: {repo}  Version: {version}")
123	
124	    pkg = f"{gar_host}/{gar_path}/{lodes_root}/{stamp}"
125	    print(f"Lode package: {pkg}")
126	
127	    token = get_pull_token(registry, repo)
128	
129	    # The top reference must be a multi-arch index — podvm families publish one index
130	    # per version since 5.4 (memo-20260608 §3.2). A single image here means the
131	    # version/family pairing is wrong; fail loud rather than capture a non-leaf.
132	    index = fetch_manifest(registry, repo, version, token, ACCEPT_INDEX)
133	    media = index.get("mediaType", "")
134	    if "image.index" not in media and "manifest.list" not in media:
135	        die(f"{family}:{version} is not an index (mediaType: {media or 'absent'})")
136	    descriptors = index.get("manifests", [])
137	
138	    acquired_at = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).strftime(
139	        "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"
140	    )
141	    rows = []
142	    members = []
143	
144	    # SELECTION is space-separated disktype:arch. Match on the index child DESCRIPTOR
145	    # (platform.architecture in the alt spelling x86_64/aarch64 + annotations.disktype),
146	    # never the layer filename — unreliable per memo-20260608 §3.4. Match the curated
147	    # set LITERALLY; never normalize arch spellings.
148	    for entry in selection.split():
149	        if ":" not in entry:
150	            die(f"malformed selection entry '{entry}' (want disktype:arch)")
151	        disktype, arch = entry.split(":", 1)
152	        member_tag = f"{sprue}{disktype}-{arch}"
153	        print(f"--- Selecting {disktype}/{arch} -> :{member_tag} ---")
154	
155	        leaf_digest = None
156	        for m in descriptors:
157	            plat = m.get("platform") or {}
158	            ann = m.get("annotations") or {}
159	            if plat.get("architecture") == arch and ann.get("disktype", "") == disktype:
160	                leaf_digest = m.get("digest")
161	                break
162	        if not leaf_digest:
163	            # Die listing every available (architecture, disktype) pair, so a quay
164	            # rotation reads as a clear inventory diff, not a mystery.
165	            available = sorted({
166	                ((m.get("platform") or {}).get("architecture", ""),
167	                 (m.get("annotations") or {}).get("disktype", ""))
168	                for m in descriptors
169	            })
170	            die(f"no leaf for {disktype}/{arch} in {family}:{version}; "
171	                f"available (architecture, disktype): {available}")
172	
173	        # Each disk leaf is a single-platform OCI artifact: empty config + exactly ONE
174	        # zstd blob. More than one layer means the descriptor matched a non-disk
175	        # manifest — die loud rather than residency-check one blob and ignore the rest.
176	        leaf = fetch_manifest(registry, repo, leaf_digest, token, ACCEPT_MANIFEST)
177	        layers = leaf.get("layers") or []
178	        if len(layers) != 1:
179	            die(f"leaf {disktype}/{arch} has {len(layers)} layers (expected 1 disk blob)")
180	        blob_digest = layers[0].get("digest")
181	        blob_size = layers[0].get("size")
182	        if not blob_digest or blob_size is None:
183	            die(f"leaf {disktype}/{arch} missing layer digest/size")
184	        print(f"  leaf manifest: {leaf_digest}")
185	        print(f"  disk blob:     {blob_digest} ({blob_size} bytes)")
186	
187	        rows.append(f"{member_tag}|{leaf_digest}|{blob_digest}|{blob_size}")
188	        # Recorded grade: the attestation IS the captured leaf digest (the `digest`
189	        # field); origin keeps the declared family:version so the rotating-upstream
190	        # provenance survives in the envelope. verification "recorded" never implies
191	        # the bytes remain re-checkable against a vanished source.
192	        members.append({
193	            "name": member_tag,
194	            "origin": f"{family}:{version}",
195	            "digest": leaf_digest,
196	            "verification": "recorded",
197	            "tags": [member_tag],
198	        })
199	
200	    if not rows:
201	        die("selection produced no leaves — nothing to immure")
202	
203	    envelope = {
204	        "schema": require_env("_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA"),
205	        "kind": brand,
206	        "lode": stamp,
207	        "acquired_at": acquired_at,
208	        "acquired_by": require_env("_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY"),
209	        "capture_build": os.environ.get("BUILD_ID", ""),
210	        "trust_grade": require_env("_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE"),
211	        "signature": None,
212	        "members": members,
213	    }
214	
215	    # Truncate-then-write (idempotent under GCB retry — CBi_103; no append mode).
216	    with open("/workspace/immure_selection.txt", "w") as f:
217	        f.write("\n".join(rows) + "\n")
218	    with open(f"/workspace/lode_{stamp}_vouch.json", "w") as f:
219	        json.dump(envelope, f)
220	    with open("/workspace/lode_stamps.txt", "w") as f:
221	        f.write(stamp + "\n")
222	
223	    # Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
224	    # immure produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package). The spine
225	    # extracts buildStepOutputs from THIS step (index 0); pin the extract slot to 0.
226	    result = {"slot_1": {"stamp": stamp, "vouch": envelope}}
227	    os.makedirs("/builder/outputs", exist_ok=True)
228	    with open("/builder/outputs/output", "w") as f:
229	        f.write(json.dumps(result))
230	
231	    print("=== Writing selection results ===")
232	    print("\n".join(rows))
233	    print("=== Immure select step complete ===")
234	
235	
236	if __name__ == "__main__":
237	    main()
238

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
3	# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, ambient GAR auth)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
6	#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
7	#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
8	#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
9	#
10	# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: read the upstream manifest, measure the
11	# canonical digest, gcrane cp into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the
12	# member tags by gcrane tag (registry-side, no blob round-trip). Author the
13	# provenance envelope and stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for
14	# the host capture-file via /builder/outputs/output. gcrane authenticates GAR
15	# ambiently via its google.Keychain (Mason SA, GCE metadata server) — no token.
16	#
17	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
18	# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
19	#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
20	#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
21	#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
22	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="
26	
27	# No token fetch: gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently through its google.Keychain
28	# (ADC -> GCE metadata server, the Mason SA). No --creds on any gcrane command.
29	
30	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
31	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
32	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
33	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
34	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
35	SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
36	SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
37	SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"
38	
39	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
40	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
41	
42	# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
43	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
44	
45	# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
46	RESULT='{'
47	FIRST=true
48	
49	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
50	  case "${SLOT}" in
51	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
52	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
53	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
54	  esac
55	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
56	  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }
57	
58	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"
59	
60	  # Read the upstream manifest, take the canonical digest, derive the glance
61	  # fingerprint — shared snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
62	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
63	#@rbgjs_include gcrane-fingerprint
64	
65	  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
66	  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
67	
68	  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
69	  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
70	  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"
71	
72	  # Collision guard — never silently clobber an existing Lode. The bole handle
73	  # (:rbi_bole) always points at this touchmark's captured image, so read it:
74	  # absent => a fresh touchmark; present => re-use. Re-use is legitimate ONLY when
75	  # it is the identical canonical digest (a Cloud Build retry re-copying the same
76	  # bytes); a different digest under the same touchmark is a clobber and fails loud.
77	  # The check sits immediately before the copy in this same step — atomic with the
78	  # GAR write in the sense the spec requires (no host pre-submit window). EXISTING_SHA
79	  # is computed the same way as SHA (sha256sum of the raw manifest), so the compare is
80	  # apples-to-apples. A manifest-read failure is treated as absent: cloud-side
81	  # auth/network is reliable, and a genuine GAR outage fails the copy below regardless.
82	  EXISTING_RAW="/workspace/ensconce_existing_${SLOT}.json"
83	  if gcrane manifest "${PKG}:${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" > "${EXISTING_RAW}" 2>/dev/null; then
84	    EXISTING_SHA=$(sha256sum "${EXISTING_RAW}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
85	    if [ "${EXISTING_SHA}" = "${SHA}" ]; then
86	      echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} already holds identical digest sha256:${SHA} — idempotent retry, proceeding."
87	    else
88	      echo "FATAL: touchmark collision at ${PKG}" >&2
89	      echo "  existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE} -> sha256:${EXISTING_SHA}" >&2
90	      echo "  refusing to clobber with  -> sha256:${SHA}" >&2
91	      echo "  (banish the Lode first, or ensconce under a fresh touchmark)" >&2
92	      exit 1
93	    fi
94	  else
95	    echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} is fresh (no existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE})."
96	  fi
97	
98	  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag. gcrane cp
99	  # copies the whole reference (manifest list and all per-platform manifests for a
100	  # multi-arch index) and preserves the manifest digest byte-for-byte.
101	  gcrane cp "${ORIGIN}" "${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
102	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
103	
104	  # Apply remaining member tags with gcrane tag — a registry-side re-tag of the
105	  # already-copied manifest, no blob round-trip (replaces the GAR->GAR copy).
106	  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
107	    gcrane tag "${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" "${MEMBER_TAG}" \
108	      || { echo "FATAL: gcrane tag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
109	  done
110	
111	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"
112	
113	  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
114	  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
115	  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
116	  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
117	  ENVELOPE='{'
118	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
119	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
120	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
121	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
122	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
123	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
124	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
125	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
126	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
127	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
128	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
129	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
130	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
131	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
132	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
133	
134	  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
135	  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
136	  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
137	
138	  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
139	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
140	    FIRST=false
141	  else
142	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
143	  fi
144	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
145	done
146	
147	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
148	
149	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
150	echo "${RESULT}"
151	
152	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
153	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
154	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
155	
156	echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="
157

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 03: Conclave the build-tool cohort into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
3	# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, always pullable —
4	#          conclave captures the reliquary tools themselves, so it cannot bootstrap
5	#          from a reliquary; gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently via its google.Keychain
6	#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
7	#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh for the orchestration. Auth canon: RBSCB)
8	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
9	#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
10	#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
11	#
12	# Pull each build-tool image from upstream, tag it into ONE GAR package
13	# rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean member tag :rbi_<tool>, and push. Author the
14	# batch provenance envelope (members[] one per tool — the cardinality axis) and
15	# stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
16	# /builder/outputs/output. Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB
17	# steps on amd64 workers; gcrane cp --platform linux/amd64 copies just the amd64
18	# manifest registry->registry (daemonless — no docker daemon, no pull/tag/push).
19	#
20	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
21	# Member tags on that package, each a distinct tool manifest:
22	#   :<TAG_SPRUE><tool>   e.g. rbi_gcloud, rbi_skopeo   (clean scheme — no digest layer)
23	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	echo "=== Conclave build-tool cohort into a Lode ==="
27	
28	STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
29	test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }
30	
31	PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
32	echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
33	
34	# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
35	# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
36	# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
37	# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
38	# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
39	# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
40	# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
41	# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
42	# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
43	# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.
44	
45	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole cohort.
46	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
47	
48	# Accumulate the envelope members[] as we capture (one element per tool). No jq
49	# dependency — values are controlled (tool name, upstream ref, hex digest, SA
50	# email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
51	MEMBERS=''
52	MFIRST=true
53	
54	while IFS='|' read -r NAME UPSTREAM; do
55	  test -n "${NAME}" || continue
56	  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE}${NAME}"
57	  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
58	
59	  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST} ---"
60	
61	  # gcrane cp copies registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push).
62	  # --platform linux/amd64 flattens a multi-arch upstream to the SINGLE amd64 manifest.
63	  # This is conclave-ONLY and must not leak to other Lode kinds (bole/wsl keep full-
64	  # fidelity capture): the reliquary cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on
65	  # amd64 workers, so the other platforms are dead bytes — and a full multi-arch index
66	  # makes the Lode package a parent-index/child-manifest web, where GAR refuses to
67	  # delete a child while its parent index exists (FAILED_PRECONDITION, "referenced by
68	  # parent manifests") and a single packages.delete removes nothing — banish then
69	  # needs multiple convergence rounds instead of one. References: RBSLC, RBSLB, RBSCB.
70	  gcrane --platform linux/amd64 cp "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
71	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }
72	
73	  # Record the upstream manifest-list digest for the envelope. gcrane digest (no
74	  # --platform) streams the tag's stored index digest (sha256:...) — the same canonical
75	  # value docker's RepoDigests reported, unchanged by the single-platform copy above, so
76	  # recorded digests stay identical to the pre-eviction docker path. CBb_101 applies.
77	  DIGEST=$(gcrane digest "${UPSTREAM}") \
78	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane digest failed for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
79	  test -n "${DIGEST}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
80	
81	  echo "${NAME} captured: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"
82	
83	  if [ "${MFIRST}" = "true" ]; then MFIRST=false; else MEMBERS="${MEMBERS},"; fi
84	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}{"
85	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
86	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"origin\":\"${UPSTREAM}\","
87	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\","
88	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
89	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
90	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}}"
91	done <<'MANIFEST'
92	gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest
93	docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest
94	alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest
95	syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest
96	binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest
97	skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest
98	gcrane|gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug
99	MANIFEST
100	
101	# Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and
102	# the host capture-file). members[] is the cardinality axis — N for the reliquary
103	# cohort, where bole carries 1.
104	ENVELOPE='{'
105	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
106	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"reliquary\","
107	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
108	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
109	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
110	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
111	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
112	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
113	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[${MEMBERS}]}"
114	
115	# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
116	# stamps file is the step-02 contract; conclave produces exactly one Lode.
117	printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
118	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
119	echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
120	
121	# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
122	# conclave produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package).
123	RESULT="{\"slot_1\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"
124	
125	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
126	echo "${RESULT}"
127	
128	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
129	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
130	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
131	
132	echo "=== Conclave capture step complete ==="
133

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 04: Fetch + GPG-verify a WSL rootfs and stage it for the wrap step
3	# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (Google-hosted, always pullable; Debian-based
4	#          — carries curl, and gnupg is apt-installed by the gpg-verify-sums snippet
5	#          if absent). The acquisition tool is curl over HTTPS, not a registry pull,
6	#          so this kind needs neither skopeo nor a reliquary bootstrap. This step does
7	#          NOT push: it fetches, verifies, authors the envelope, and stages rootfs.tar
8	#          for the gcrane-append wrap (rbgjl05) — the fetch/verify builder (curl+gpg,
9	#          Debian) and the wrap builder (gcrane:debug busybox) cannot be one image.
10	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
11	#                _RBGL_TAG_ROOTFS, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
12	#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY, _RBGL_WSL_URL, _RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR
13	#
14	# Fetch the vendor rootfs tarball (URL assembled host-side from the version args)
15	# over HTTPS, DISCOVER its checksum from Canonical's published, GPG-signed
16	# SHA256SUMS (verified against the pinned signing-key fingerprint — the
17	# verified-against-published gate), verify the rootfs bytes against it, then stage the
18	# OPAQUE tarball for the wrap step (rbgjl05), which gcrane-appends it as a single-layer
19	# OCI member (never extracted) into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean
20	# member tag :rbi_rootfs. Author the batch provenance envelope (members[]
21	# length 1 — the cardinality axis) and stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch
22	# artifact) and for the host capture-file via /builder/outputs/output.
23	# Single-platform (linux/amd64): the rootfs is an opaque blob, not a multi-arch
24	# manifest. No digest is pinned here — the published checksum is discovered and
25	# signature-verified at capture, per the no-FQIN premise (RBSLU).
26	#
27	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
28	# Member tag on that package (clean scheme — no digest/fingerprint layer):
29	#   :<TAG_ROOTFS>   e.g. rbi_rootfs   (the opaque rootfs blob)
30	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
31	#
32	# Trust grade verified-against-published: the member digest recorded in the
33	# envelope is the tarball's GPG-verified published SHA-256, tagged verification
34	# "gpg-sha256-published" with the signing-key fingerprint — NOT an OCI manifest
35	# digest, because what is attested is capture-fidelity of an opaque blob under the
36	# vendor's signed checksum.
37	
38	set -euo pipefail
39	echo "=== Underpin WSL rootfs into a Lode ==="
40	
41	STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
42	test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }
43	
44	URL="${_RBGL_WSL_URL}"
45	KEY_FPR="${_RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR}"
46	test -n "${URL}"     || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_WSL_URL missing" >&2; exit 1; }
47	test -n "${KEY_FPR}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR missing" >&2; exit 1; }
48	
49	PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
50	MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_ROOTFS}"
51	DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
52	echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
53	echo "Source:       ${URL}"
54	
55	# Acquisition moment, attested for the single-member cohort.
56	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
57	
58	# --- Discover the published checksum under the pinned signing key ---
59	# The sums + signature live beside the tarball; the target filename is its
60	# basename. The snippet GPG-verifies the sums and provides EXPECTED_SHA.
61	SUMS_URL="${URL%/*}/SHA256SUMS"
62	SIG_URL="${SUMS_URL}.gpg"
63	TARGET_BASENAME="${URL##*/}"
64	#@rbgjs_include gpg-verify-sums
65	
66	# --- Fetch the rootfs over HTTPS (cloud-side; the workstation never touches these
67	# bytes — only the URL was assembled host-side) ---
68	ROOTFS="/workspace/underpin_${STAMP}.rootfs.tar"
69	echo "--- Fetching rootfs tarball ---"
70	curl -fSL --retry 3 -o "${ROOTFS}" "${URL}" \
71	  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to fetch rootfs from ${URL}" >&2; exit 1; }
72	
73	# --- Verify the rootfs bytes against the GPG-verified published checksum ---
74	# Tool availability on the docker builder is a Palisade: sha256sum (coreutils) is
75	# the expected path; shasum / openssl are honest fallbacks so a missing tool fails
76	# the compare loud rather than skipping verification.
77	echo "--- Verifying rootfs against published checksum ---"
78	if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
79	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(sha256sum "${ROOTFS}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
80	elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
81	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(shasum -a 256 "${ROOTFS}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
82	elif command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
83	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(openssl dgst -sha256 "${ROOTFS}" | awk '{print $NF}')
84	else
85	  echo "FATAL: no sha256 tool (sha256sum/shasum/openssl) on builder — cannot verify" >&2
86	  exit 1
87	fi
88	test -n "${ACTUAL_SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest computed for rootfs" >&2; exit 1; }
89	
90	if [ "${ACTUAL_SHA}" != "${EXPECTED_SHA}" ]; then
91	  echo "FATAL: rootfs checksum mismatch — refusing to capture unverified bytes" >&2
92	  echo "  published (GPG-verified) sha256: ${EXPECTED_SHA}" >&2
93	  echo "  fetched   sha256:                ${ACTUAL_SHA}" >&2
94	  exit 1
95	fi
96	echo "Verified: sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA} (matches GPG-signed published checksum)"
97	
98	# --- Stage the verified OPAQUE tarball for the wrap step (rbgjl05) ---
99	# The rootfs is staged byte-for-byte into a context dir holding ONLY rootfs.tar;
100	# rbgjl05 gcrane-appends that dir as a FROM-scratch single-layer member (rootfs.tar at
101	# image root, never extracted — gcrane append wraps the blob, it does not unpack it).
102	# The wrap is a separate step because gcrane:debug busybox has no curl/gpg: this Debian
103	# builder fetches and verifies, the gcrane builder wraps and pushes.
104	echo "--- Staging verified rootfs for the wrap step -> ${DEST} ---"
105	CTX="/workspace/underpin_ctx_${STAMP}"
106	mkdir -p "${CTX}"
107	cp "${ROOTFS}" "${CTX}/rootfs.tar"
108	
109	echo "Rootfs verified and staged: ${DEST} (sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA})"
110	
111	# --- Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch
112	# and the host capture-file). members[] length 1 — the wsl singleton. No jq
113	# dependency — values are controlled (member tag, URL, hex digest, key fingerprint,
114	# SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote. ---
115	ENVELOPE='{'
116	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
117	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"wsl\","
118	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
119	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
120	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
121	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
122	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
123	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
124	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
125	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
126	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${URL}\","
127	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA}\","
128	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"gpg-sha256-published\","
129	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signing_key\":\"${KEY_FPR}\","
130	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
131	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
132	
133	# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
134	# stamps file is the step-02 contract; underpin produces exactly one Lode.
135	printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
136	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
137	echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
138	
139	# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
140	# underpin produces exactly one Lode.
141	RESULT="{\"slot_1\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"
142	
143	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
144	echo "${RESULT}"
145	
146	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
147	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
148	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
149	
150	echo "=== Underpin capture step complete ==="
151

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
3	# Builder: gcrane (pinned reliquary gcrane for bole; floating bootstrap gcrane for
4	#          conclave/wsl — the recipe row in each per-kind body sets which)
5	# Entrypoint: busybox (gcrane:debug's only shell)
6	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
7	#
8	# Note: this script runs inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module
9	# discipline (CBG governs).
10	#
11	# The capture step (rbgjl01 ensconce / rbgjl03 conclave / rbgjl04 underpin) staged one
12	# envelope per captured Lode at /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json and listed the
13	# stamps in /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as
14	# the captured manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
15	# Vouch content is architecture-independent; the single appended layer is sufficient.
16	
17	set -euo pipefail
18	
19	echo "=== Assemble and push Lode vouch artifacts ==="
20	
21	test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
22	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
23	test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
24	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }
25	
26	test -n "${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH missing" >&2; exit 1; }
27	
28	while IFS= read -r STAMP || test -n "${STAMP}"; do
29	  test -n "${STAMP}" || continue
30	
31	  ENVELOPE_FILE="/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
32	  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \
33	    || { echo "FATAL: envelope not staged for ${STAMP}: ${ENVELOPE_FILE}" >&2; exit 1; }
34	
35	  VOUCH_URI="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}"
36	  echo "--- Vouch for ${STAMP} -> ${VOUCH_URI} ---"
37	
38	  # Stage the envelope alone in a context dir; gcrane append wraps it as a FROM-scratch
39	  # single-layer member (vouch.json lands at image root).
40	  CTX="/workspace/vouch_ctx_${STAMP}"
41	  mkdir -p "${CTX}"
42	  cp "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" "${CTX}/vouch.json"
43	
44	  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch layer via gcrane append — Lode-family snippet.
45	  APPEND_CTX="${CTX}"
46	  APPEND_URI="${VOUCH_URI}"
47	#@rbgjs_include gcrane-append
48	
49	  echo "Vouch pushed: ${VOUCH_URI}"
50	done < /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
51	
52	echo "=== Vouch push step complete ==="
53

2	#

4	#
