12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - bole body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   ensconce — capture an upstream base image into a Lode (Director credentials)
21	# The bole rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
22	# kind-specific data — the ensconce recipe (gcrane capture + vouch-push),
23	# the substitutions blob, and the touchmark-fact extract — and composes them
24	# through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
25	# step-composition machinery lives here.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	# Ensconce is capture-pure: it writes no consumer config. It hands the captured
30	# touchmark to a later derived-pull election (the conjure ANCHOR populator)
31	# through two bare single-form chaining facts (RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK +
32	# RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND) via the depth-1 cross-tabtarget chain. The provenance
33	# envelope lives only in GAR (:rbi_vouch tag, pushed cloud-side by rbgjl02),
34	# never host-side.
35	
36	######################################################################
37	# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)
38	
39	# Internal: compose the ensconce capture recipe (gcrane capture + vouch-push) and
40	# its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit and
41	# poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
42	# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
43	# enshrine-borrowed poll ceiling.
44	# Args: token origin stamp
45	zrbld_ensconce_submit() {
46	  zrbld_sentinel
47	
48	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
49	  local -r z_origin="${2:?Origin required}"
50	  local -r z_stamp="${3:?Stamp required}"
51	
52	  buc_step "Constructing ensconce capture recipe"
53	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
54	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
55	
56	  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
57	  # spine. The | delimiter is load-bearing — builder refs carry colons (tags).
58	  # bole is a sealed-reliquary consumer: BOTH steps ride the PINNED reliquary gcrane
59	  # (z_rbfc_tool_gcrane), never the floating bootstrap — zero unpinned aspects.
60	  local -r z_recipe=(
61	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl01-ensconce-capture.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_gcrane}|ensconce-capture|busybox"
62	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_gcrane}|assemble-push-vouch|busybox"
63	  )
64	
65	  buc_log_args "Composing ensconce substitutions blob"
66	  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}subs.json"
67	  jq -n \
68	    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
69	    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
70	    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
71	    --arg zjq_tag_bole     "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE}" \
72	    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
73	    --arg zjq_tag_digest   "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}" \
74	    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
75	    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
76	    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
77	    --arg zjq_origin_1     "${z_origin}" \
78	    --arg zjq_stamp_1      "${z_stamp}" \
79	    '{
80	      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:          $zjq_gar_host,
81	      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:          $zjq_gar_path,
82	      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:        $zjq_lodes_root,
83	      _RBGL_TAG_BOLE:          $zjq_tag_bole,
84	      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:         $zjq_tag_vouch,
85	      _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX: $zjq_tag_digest,
86	      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:       $zjq_trust_grade,
87	      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA:      $zjq_vouch_schema,
88	      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:       $zjq_acquired_by,
89	      _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN:    $zjq_origin_1,
90	      _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN:    "",
91	      _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN:    "",
92	      _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP:      $zjq_stamp_1,
93	      _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP:      "",
94	      _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP:      ""
95	    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
96	    || buc_die "Failed to compose ensconce substitutions blob"
97	
98	  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
99	    "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "Ensconce" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_ENSHRINE}" \
100	    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}" \
101	    "${z_recipe[@]}"
102	}
103	
104	# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed ensconce build and
105	# emit the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand
106	# enum). The gcrane capture step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the
107	# host-minted stamp per slot; the vouch-push step writes no output. Base-kind
108	# ensconce captures exactly one base, so exactly one slot is populated — the
109	# single-form facts are one-per-dispatch and buf_write_fact_single's no-clobber
110	# guard turns any second populated slot into a loud failure. The provenance
111	# envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR (rbgjl02 pushed it under
112	# :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the touchmark a consumer needs.
113	zrbld_ensconce_extract() {
114	  zrbld_sentinel
115	
116	  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"
117	
118	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}output.json"
119	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
120	
121	  buc_log_args "Ensconce output:"
122	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"
123	
124	  local z_n=""
125	  local z_slot_key=""
126	  local z_stamp_file=""
127	  local z_stamp=""
128	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
129	    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
130	    z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_ENSCONCE_PREFIX}${z_n}_stamp.txt"
131	    jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.stamp // empty" "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
132	      || buc_die "Failed to read stamp for ${z_slot_key}"
133	    z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
134	    test -n "${z_stamp}" || continue
135	
136	    buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
137	      || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
138	    buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE}" \
139	      || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
140	    buc_success "Ensconced Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE})"
141	  done
142	}
143	
144	######################################################################
145	# External Functions (rbld_*)
146	
147	rbld_ensconce() {
148	  zrbld_sentinel
149	
150	  buc_doc_brief "Ensconce an upstream base image into a Lode (parallel rbi_ld capture)"
151	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory declaring the base ORIGIN"
152	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
153	
154	  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load.
155	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
156	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
157	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
158	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
159	
160	  # Resolve the single base ORIGIN. Base-kind ensconce captures one base per
161	  # Lode per invocation; multi-base vessels are dispatched per base. Every real
162	  # vessel declares exactly one base slot today.
163	  local z_origin=""
164	  local z_origin_count=0
165	  local z_n=""
166	  local z_origin_var=""
167	  local z_slot_origin=""
168	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
169	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
170	    z_slot_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
171	    test -n "${z_slot_origin}" || continue
172	    z_origin="${z_slot_origin}"
173	    z_origin_count=$((z_origin_count + 1))
174	  done
175	
176	  test "${z_origin_count}" -ne 0 \
177	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slot (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"
178	  test "${z_origin_count}" -eq 1 \
179	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares ${z_origin_count} base slots; base-kind ensconce captures one base per Lode — invoke per base"
180	
181	  # Reject producer-vessel pins: an origin naming a local vessel directory is a
182	  # made-side hallmark-pin (bind/airgap), not an upstream base to capture.
183	  test ! -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}" \
184	    || buc_die "Origin '${z_origin}' names a producer vessel — base-Lode capture is for upstream bases, not hallmark-pins"
185	
186	  buc_info "Ensconce base: ${z_origin}"
187	
188	  # Resolve tool images from the reliquary. bole is a sealed-reliquary consumer, so
189	  # BOTH steps ride the PINNED reliquary gcrane (z_rbfc_tool_gcrane) — zero unpinned
190	  # aspects (RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary). The vessel supplies RBRV_RELIQUARY; gcrane
191	  # auths GAR ambiently whether pulled from gcr.io or our AR.
192	  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images
193	
194	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
195	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
196	
197	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
198	  local z_token=""
199	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
200	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
201	
202	  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
203	  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
204	  local z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
205	
206	  # Tweak override: test infrastructure pins the stamp via the bure tweak channel
207	  # to drive two captures onto one touchmark (the collision-guard exercise) — the
208	  # time-based mint is seconds-grained, so two CLI ensconces never share a
209	  # touchmark and the cloud guard's idempotent/collision branches never fire
210	  # without a pin. The name carries the buo sprue (BURE enforces the shape).
211	  # Mirror: rbtdrc_crucible.rs RBTDRC_ENSCONCE_STAMP_TWEAK_NAME — same literal.
212	  local -r z_ensconce_stamp_tweak_name="buorb_ensconce_stamp"
213	  test "${BURE_TWEAK_NAME:-}" != "${z_ensconce_stamp_tweak_name}" || z_stamp="${BURE_TWEAK_VALUE}"
214	
215	  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
216	
217	  zrbld_ensconce_submit "${z_token}" "${z_origin}" "${z_stamp}"
218	  zrbld_ensconce_extract
219	
220	  buc_success "Ensconce complete: ${z_origin} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
221	}
222	
223	# eof
224

content.text: Now let me read the memo and the ACG file:

content.content:
1	# Memo: Lode podvm capture — cerebro experiment (₢BHAAK)
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-06-08
4	**Heat / pace:** ₣BH (rbk-11-mvp-lode-universal-capture) / ₢BHAAK (lode-podvm-cerebro-experiment)
5	**Venue:** cerebro (Linux x86_64, Ubuntu 24.04, glibc 2.39) — by express, throwaway exception (RBK workstation is bash/curl/openssl/jq only and must stay that way).
6	**Author:** Claude Opus 4.8, driven by Brad.
7	
8	This memo is the durable record of an experiment whose physical evidence (scratch
9	binaries, captured GAR Lodes) is destroyed at teardown by design. It establishes
10	how Recipe Bottle should bring podman-VM machine-os images under control in GAR.
11	It does **not** build the production cloud capture — that is a separate follow-up
12	pace this experiment unblocks.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## 1. Bottom line (the cloud-layer decision)
17	
18	- **Tool: `crane`** for the production cloud capture step. Both crane and oras copy
19	  the podvm artifact with **perfect digest fidelity**; skopeo is confirmed out.
20	  crane wins on: Google's own go-containerregistry (already proven in the retired
21	  RBSOB OCI-layout bridge and across our GCB/GAR), `crane cp`-by-digest is
22	  get-or-error (the loud failure the *recorded* trust grade wants), and it handles
23	  both the OCI-artifact disk leaves **and** ordinary container images uniformly.
24	  **oras is a fully-viable fallback** (artifact-native, also digest-faithful) and
25	  is the better tool if the step ever needs `oras discover`/referrers semantics.
26	- **Auth: `gcrane`, not plain `crane` — ambient Google keychain, zero token
27	  plumbing.** The cloud step uses **`gcrane`** (crane's Google-auth sibling:
28	  identical `cp`/`manifest`/`tag` engine, same go-containerregistry repo) from
29	  `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug` (the `:debug` variant carries a
30	  busybox shell for the bash orchestration). gcrane authenticates via
31	  `google.Keychain`, which matches `*.pkg.dev` (our GAR) and draws credentials
32	  from Application Default Credentials → the GCE metadata server — so on Cloud
33	  Build it auths to GAR ambiently as the Mason SA with **no `crane auth login`, no
34	  in-memory token-fetch, no credential-helper image**. Plain `crane` uses only the
35	  docker-config keychain and would need the explicit-login + token dance; the Cloud
36	  SDK image does not bundle crane at all. This generalizes conclave's existing
37	  ambient model (`gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`, no explicit login) from docker to
38	  crane. Full evidence in §9.
39	- **skopeo stays ruled out — but the recorded rationale was half-wrong.** The cinch
40	  ruled it out for *silently skipping foreign/non-distributable layers* (issue 545).
41	  That failure mode **does not apply** to this content: the disk blobs are
42	  `application/zstd`, a **distributable** media type, not `nondistributable`.
43	  skopeo's *actual* disqualifier is that its cooked operations **fatal** on the
44	  empty-config OCI artifact: `unsupported image-specific operation on artifact with
45	  type "application/vnd.oci.empty.v1+json"`. So skopeo fails **loud, not silent** —
46	  still wrong for the job, but for the issue-1608 (strict artifact handling) family,
47	  not the issue-545 (silent skip) family. Update the spec rationale accordingly.
48	- **Capture by *leaf digest*, never by copying the whole tag/index.** The upstream
49	  tag is a multi-arch OCI index that **mixes** real container images and disk
50	  artifacts (native family) and spans 5–15 GB. The step must read the index, select
51	  specific `{disktype × arch}` leaves, and `crane cp <repo>@<leaf-digest>` each one.
52	- **Anti-hollow-mirror guard is a host-side curl, no image tool.** A GAR registry
53	  v2 blob `HEAD` returns `Content-Length`; assert it equals the manifest's declared
54	  layer size. Proven here against 1.13 GB / 245 MB / 200 MB / 194 MB blobs. This is
55	  exactly the cloud-side-curl shape the recorded grade demands.
56	- **Trust grade: `recorded-at-acquisition`**, confirmed appropriate. quay rotates
57	  podvm images out within days and publishes no durable checksum; RB attests the
58	  digest observed at capture (trust-on-first-acquisition).
59	
60	---
61	
62	## 2. Environment & tools (exact versions)
63	
64	| Tool | Version | Acquisition | Linkage |
65	|------|---------|-------------|---------|
66	| crane | **v0.21.6** | `google/go-containerregistry` release, `go-containerregistry_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz` | static ELF |
67	| oras | **v1.3.2** | `oras-project/oras` release, `oras_1.3.2_linux_amd64.tar.gz` | static ELF |
68	| skopeo | **1.22.2** | `quay.io/skopeo/stable@sha256:c7d3c512612f52805023cd38351081dad7e2729fc13d14b701e47c7c8bdd6615` via docker (no official static binary exists) | container |
69	| docker (host) | 29.5.3 | pre-installed on cerebro | — |
70	
71	All three image tools were installed into a single throwaway scratch dir
72	(`~/lode-podvm-scratch/bin`); skopeo as a thin docker-wrapper script. Teardown
73	removes the scratch dir and `docker rmi quay.io/skopeo/stable`.
74	
75	**Credential note:** the experiment used the **Director** identity `canest-dir`
76	(`director-canest-dir@cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001.iam.gserviceaccount.com`). The
77	Director RBRA already resided on cerebro from prior work. A self-contained
78	throwaway token-mint (`mint-token.sh`, SA JWT-bearer flow via openssl+curl) issued
79	the GAR OAuth token, independent of the project kindle — this is also a useful
80	finding (see §6). Post-pace credential hygiene (payor reauth + governor remantle)
81	is the operator's, per the cinch.
82	
83	---
84	
85	## 3. Upstream characterization (the core finding)
86	
87	Anchor version **podman 5.6** (latest version common to both families).
88	
89	### 3.1 Provenance anchors (quay index digests at 2026-06-08)
90	
91	| Family | Tag | Index digest |
92	|--------|-----|--------------|
93	| native `quay.io/podman/machine-os` | `5.6` | `sha256:6aa8bfeb41a8ce76b52277f5a609e5adeb097725ca4c3591c8ce2b4f4446dd47` |
94	| wsl `quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl` | `5.6` | `sha256:61bb25d36420d8eedf240cbb12deee90b6ff6e31e2d5aba224f537f8bcafdb05` |
95	
96	(quay rotates podvm; these index digests are valid as observed on 2026-06-08 and
97	may age out within days — the reason the trust grade is *recorded*.)
98	
99	### 3.2 Tag landscape — the families diverge
100	
101	- **native** carries `5.0 … 6.0` + `next`. Versions `5.0–5.3` also publish per-arch
102	  tags (`5.x-amd64`, `5.x-arm64`); **at 5.4 the per-arch tags were dropped** — a
103	  single multi-arch index per version since.
104	- **wsl** carries `5.3–5.6` only. No per-arch tags. **Lags the native family.**
105	- **Consequence:** a capture pipeline must pin **per-family** version + scheme;
106	  it cannot assume version parity or a stable tag convention across families.
107	
108	### 3.3 Manifest structure
109	
110	Both top-level tags are `application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json` (schemaVersion 2,
111	no top-level `artifactType`).
112	
113	- native:5.6 → **10** child manifests, **two heterogeneous axes**:
114	  - 2 **plain** children `{arm64, amd64}`, no disktype → **real container OS images**
115	    (config `oci.image.config.v1+json`, ~65 normal `tar+gzip` layers).
116	  - 8 **disktype** children `{aarch64, x86_64} × {applehv, hyperv, qemu, wsl}`,
117	    disktype carried in `annotations["disktype"]`, arch in the alt spelling
118	    (`aarch64`/`x86_64`, vs `arm64`/`amd64` on the plain children).
119	- wsl:5.6 → **2** child manifests, `wsl` disktype × `{x86_64, aarch64}`.
120	- The native family's wsl-disktype leaves are **distinct digests** from the
121	  standalone wsl family's — one family does not characterize the other.
122	
123	### 3.4 Leaf shapes
124	
125	| Leaf class | mediaType | config | layers |
126	|---|---|---|---|
127	| **disktype** (qemu/wsl/applehv/hyperv) | `oci.image.manifest.v1+json` | `oci.empty.v1+json` (2 bytes) | **single** `application/zstd` blob; filename in `org.opencontainers.image.title` |
128	| **plain** (amd64/arm64) | oci manifest | `oci.image.config.v1+json` | ~65 `tar+gzip` layers (genuine container image) |
129	
130	- The disk artifacts are **OCI artifacts** (empty-config sentinel + one arbitrary
131	  compressed blob), **not** container images — the modern `oras push`-style shape.
132	- Blobs are `application/zstd` = **distributable**. No foreign/non-distributable
133	  markers anywhere. (This is what invalidates the issue-545 silent-skip concern.)
134	- **Annotation caveat:** the layer `image.title` is unreliable for version/arch —
135	  the 5.6 wsl-family x86_64 leaf is titled `5.0-rootfs-amd64.tar.zst` (stale
136	  version *and* alt arch spelling). **Selection must key on the index descriptor's
137	  `platform` + `annotations.disktype`, never the layer filename.**
138	
139	---
140	
141	## 4. Tool verdict (empirical)
142	
143	| Operation | crane 0.21.6 | oras 1.3.2 | skopeo 1.22.2 |
144	|---|---|---|---|
145	| read index | ✅ `crane manifest` | ✅ `oras manifest fetch` | ✅ `inspect --raw` |
146	| read artifact leaf | ✅ | ✅ (sees descriptor, 474 B) | ✅ raw only |
147	| **cooked inspect of artifact** | n/a | n/a | ❌ **FATAL** empty-config artifact |
148	| **copy artifact → GAR (digest-faithful)** | ✅ (qemu 1.13 GB, exact digest) | ✅ (245 MB, exact digest) | not attempted (ruled out) |
149	
150	`crane cp` and `oras cp` both produced a GAR copy whose manifest digest is
151	**byte-identical** to the upstream leaf digest. crane reported `pushed blob` for the
152	zstd layer and reused the well-known empty-config blob
153	(`sha256:44136fa355b3678a1146ad16f7e8649e94fb4fc21fe77e8310c060f61caaff8a`).
154	
155	---
156	
157	## 5. What was captured (and proven, then banished)
158	
159	Two Lodes in the depot GAR (`rbi_ld`, repo
160	`cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001/cancbhm-canest3bhm100001-gar`, us-central1):
161	
162	**`rbi_ld/vn260608213343`** (kind podvm-native, recorded grade):
163	
164	| member tag | tool | upstream leaf digest | size |
165	|---|---|---|---|
166	| `:rbi_qemu-x86_64` | crane cp | `sha256:a754dbe92b9171538872419734cb6b406eeb15928829f461718bf6e438bd910c` | 1,130,075,172 |
167	| `:rbi_wsl-x86_64` | oras cp | `sha256:8d03454f33c9a21079c4f3d4b1b046abc336c3e2448eb0f3563f1e3faf9f7db7` | 244,634,175 |
168	| `:rbi_vouch` | docker FROM-scratch | (provenance envelope) | — |
169	
170	**`rbi_ld/vw260608213906`** (kind podvm-wsl, recorded grade):
171	
172	| member tag | tool | upstream leaf digest | size |
173	|---|---|---|---|
174	| `:rbi_wsl-x86_64` | crane cp | `sha256:3d6f30202266fc9d6a2e2129bce3702dd489686075f24a4b2694221a8a9ae1d1` | 199,835,738 |
175	| `:rbi_wsl-aarch64` | oras cp | `sha256:0131613d9fdc688eb6688dedc394589fb99bbaa21236d87e78cf1fbf86a0b0dc` | 193,808,928 |
176	| `:rbi_vouch` | docker FROM-scratch | (provenance envelope) | — |
177	
178	Control proofs (all host-side, no image tool):
179	- **divine** (`rbw-ld`) enumerated both as `(cohort: N members)`. divine is
180	  **kind-agnostic for enumeration** — the absent `vn`/`vw` legend lines are
181	  cosmetic (the follow-up pace registers them); the Lodes list correctly anyway.
182	- **anti-hollow-mirror guard**: GAR registry-v2 blob `HEAD` returned HTTP 200 with
183	  `Content-Length` exactly equal to each manifest's declared layer size, for all
184	  four members. No hollow mirror.
185	- **banish** (`rbw-lB`) deleted a Lode (the stray, see §6) in a single
186	  `packages delete`; divine then showed it absent. The two evidence Lodes were
187	  banished at teardown (§7).
188	
189	The `:rbi_vouch` envelope used the existing `rbld-vouch-1` schema
190	(schema/kind/lode/acquired_at/acquired_by/capture_build/trust_grade/signature/
191	members[]), with `trust_grade:"recorded-at-acquisition"` and each member carrying
192	`{name, origin, digest, verification:"recorded", tags[]}`. `members[]` is the
193	cardinality axis (length 2 here), identical in shape to bole's length-1.
194	
195	---
196	
197	## 6. Process notes / spooks
198	
199	- **Credential collapse at mount.** Both Governor (`account not found`) and
200	  Director (`Invalid JWT Signature`) probes failed. Root cause: the Governor SA had
201	  been torn down; the Director key was stale downstream. Repair chain:
202	  `rbw-aM` (payor mantles governor) → `rbw-adr` (roster to confirm identity
203	  `canest-dir`) → `rbw-adI canest-dir` (governor invests director) → re-probe green.
204	  The probe retry loop **mislabels both as "SA propagation race"** even when the
205	  failure is terminal (account-not-found / invalid-signature persist all 10
206	  attempts, 104 s) — a 104 s wait for a condition that will never clear. Worth a
207	  spook: distinguish terminal IAM errors from genuine propagation races and
208	  fail-fast on the former.
209	- **Empty-stamp capture bug (mine).** A `VN_STAMP` read via `$(grep …)` under
210	  `set -u` tripped a harness shell-snapshot quirk (`ZSH_VERSION: unbound variable`)
211	  and returned empty, so an `oras cp` landed in package `rbi_ld/vn` (no stamp)
212	  instead of `rbi_ld/vn260608213343`. Caught immediately by the post-push digest
213	  check; the stray became the banish test subject. Lesson for the cloud step: the
214	  stamp must be computed once and passed as an explicit substitution, never re-read
215	  mid-pipeline.
216	- **Self-contained token mint.** `rbgo_get_token_capture` needs the full project
217	  kindle. For an off-station experiment, a 20-line openssl+curl SA-JWT-bearer mint
218	  reading only the RBRA fields was simpler and is reusable for any
219	  RBRA-on-a-foreign-host situation. Not a production path (production stays
220	  cloud-side), but a handy diagnostic seam.
221	- **skopeo via docker, not static.** No official static skopeo binary exists; the
222	  authoritative throwaway is the `quay.io/skopeo/stable` image. Cleaner than a
223	  third-party static rebuild of unknown provenance.
224	
225	---
226	
227	## 7. The follow-up cloud pace (what this unblocks)
228	
229	Build the production podvm capture **cloud-side**, riding the spine exactly like
230	underpin/conclave (do NOT touch the workstation to acquire bytes):
231	
232	1. **`rbgjl05-immure-capture.sh`** (new in-pool step, builder =
233	   `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`, ambient GAR auth via the Google
234	   keychain — see §9; entrypoint the busybox shell). It must:
235	   - read the family index at the nameplate-pinned podman version,
236	   - select the curated `{disktype × arch}` leaf set from the index **by descriptor
237	     platform+disktype** (not by layer filename),
238	   - `crane cp <family>@<leaf-digest> <GAR>/rbi_ld/<vn|vw><stamp>:rbi_<variant>`
239	     per selected leaf (by-digest, loud-fail),
240	   - run the **blob-residency guard** (registry-v2 `HEAD`, Content-Length ==
241	     declared size) before vouching,
242	   - then hand off to the existing **`rbgjl02`** vouch step unchanged.
243	2. **`rbldv_*.sh`** body — podvm is **opaque-blob × multi-member**, a blend of
244	   `rbldw_Underpin` (opaque-blob) and `rbldr_Reliquary` (multi-member cohort).
245	   It composes a spine recipe (capture step + vouch step) plus a substitutions blob
246	   carrying family, version, and the declarative selected-platform list. **One verb
247	   `immure` (`rbw-lI`)** spans both quay families via a family/archive argument
248	   (per the paddock Vocabulary), not two verbs.
249	3. **Registration surface** (the 12f48cbab spook — don't miss the single-fixture
250	   registry): `rbgc_Constants.sh` brands/tags (kind-letters `vn`/`vw` already
251	   reserved), `rbldl_Lifecycle.sh` divine legend (add vn/vw lines), and the theurge
252	   fixture registry across `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` + `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` **including
253	   `RBTDRC_FIXTURES`**.
254	4. **Theurge fixture** `podvm-lifecycle` (service tier): immure → divine
255	   (enumerate + cohort count) → inspect members + `:rbi_vouch` → per-member +
256	   whole-Lode banish → absent. Add to service + complete suites.
257	5. **Declarative intent (no FQIN).** The nameplate/host-tier config declares family
258	   + version + desired `{disktype, arch}` set; `rbgjl05` resolves leaf digests from
259	   the live index at capture time. Support a "widen selected set against the same
260	   upstream version" refresh mode, separate from "bump podman version" — quay's
261	   hostile retention can make later expansion impossible once a version ages out.
262	
263	---
264	
265	## 8. Premises confirmed / nuanced
266	
267	- **Cloud-side acquisition premise STANDS.** cerebro was a one-time lab bench; the
268	  experiment did not tempt a workstation-hosted production path.
269	- **podvm-selective retention STANDS** — captured 2 curated leaves per family, not
270	  the 5–15 GB full index.
271	- **recorded grade STANDS** and is correctly the *only* honest grade for podvm.
272	- **skopeo-out STANDS, rationale corrected** (artifact-rejection, not silent-skip;
273	  see §1). RBSPV / the Lode spec should be updated to state the real failure mode.
274	- **crane lean CONFIRMED**, with oras documented as an equal-fidelity fallback.
275	
276	---
277	
278	## 9. Cloud-side auth — gcrane ambient Google keychain (2026-06-08, source-read follow-up)
279	
280	Settled after the cerebro teardown by reading go-containerregistry source, not by
281	re-running the bench. Resolves the auth axis the bole-eviction pace cinched as
282	"decided here, inherited downstream" — the experiment used an explicit off-station
283	token mint (§2 credential note, §6 spook) and never characterized the *cloud*
284	auth path. This section is that path.
285	
286	**The binary distinction is load-bearing — `crane` and `gcrane` authenticate differently:**
287	
288	- **`crane`** authenticates only via `authn.DefaultKeychain` (the docker config
289	  file). On a Cloud Build worker that config is empty, so plain crane needs an
290	  explicit `crane auth login` fed by an in-memory metadata-server token — the
291	  skopeo token-fetch dance, ported, not removed.
292	- **`gcrane`** is the Google variant: same `cp`/`manifest`/`tag`/`digest` engine,
293	  but it authenticates through `google.Keychain`. Per `pkg/v1/google/keychain.go`,
294	  that keychain (a) matches `gcr.io`, `*.gcr.io`, **`*.pkg.dev`** (Artifact
295	  Registry — our GAR host), and `*.google.com`, and (b) draws credentials from
296	  Application Default Credentials → the **GCE metadata server** first, gcloud
297	  second, anonymous last. On a Cloud Build worker the Mason SA *is* the
298	  metadata-server identity, so gcrane auths to GAR ambiently with zero auth code.
299	- **The Cloud SDK / gcloud image does not contain crane** — it is gcloud+gsutil+bq
300	  on Debian/Alpine. "A Google image that has gcloud and presumably crane" is a
301	  false premise; that path means installing crane into a fat SDK image yourself.
302	
303	**Decision.** The bole capture step — and every later crane-embrace capture step —
304	uses **`gcrane`** from **`gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`**. The `:debug`
305	variant carries `/busybox/sh` (the bash orchestration) and busybox `sha256sum`
306	(the fingerprint); the non-debug image is distroless — no shell, unusable as a
307	script step. Pin by digest in production, not the floating `:debug` tag. The
308	in-memory `token-fetch` snippet is **dropped** from the bole path; it survives only
309	where a non-keychain tool still needs it, and retires entirely as skopeo and docker
310	are evicted.
311	
312	**This is conclave's model generalized, not a new one.** The reliquary capture step
313	(rbgjl03, RBSLC) already auths ambiently — `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`, "no
314	explicit login," cred-helper via the Mason SA. gcrane extends that same ambient
315	posture from docker to crane, so the whole capture family shares one auth story
316	instead of two. The durable canon for this lives in **RBSCB** (Cloud Build
317	posture), which today still enshrines the skopeo "cannot use the credential helper,
318	fetch the token in-memory" rationale that `token-fetch.sh` cites — that line is
319	superseded by this finding and updated as the eviction lands.
320	
321	**Sources:**
322	- go-containerregistry README — crane / gcrane / krane variants and their keychains
323	- `pkg/v1/google/keychain.go` — `isGoogle()` host matching (`*.pkg.dev`) + ADC/metadata credential acquisition
324	- crane image docs — distroless default, `:debug` busybox shell at `/busybox/sh`
325	- Cloud SDK Docker image contents — gcloud/gsutil/bq, no crane
326

120	one string; this clause gives a claim one wording. Canonical specimen: the
121	2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context file *characterized* a
122	spec's scope instead of citing it, and the characterization carried the error
123	for months.
124	
125	**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
126	sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
127	because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
128	lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
129	guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
130	implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
131	knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
132	above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.
133	
134	## The fourth clause — wire formats are named homes
135	
136	A JSON document this project authors on **both sides** — an envelope, a result
137	slot, a substitutions blob — is itself a home: a wire format with senders and
138	receivers that must stay findable from each other. Its keys are therefore
139	**minted names** (CLAUDE.md mint: any persistent name anywhere is in scope),
140	carrying one key-sprue per format: `rblv_digest`, never bare `digest`. The sprue
141	is what makes the format greppable — `grep rblv_` returns the author, every
142	consumer, and the schema definition, with no central registry to drift (the
143	`buo` tweak-sprue precedent; the `_RBGL_` substitution registers are the senior
144	worked example). One sprue per *format*, not per file or per domain: two formats
145	sharing a sprue blur the census that is the sprue's whole purpose.
146	
147	The boundary is the Palisade: JSON conforming to a **foreign** schema — a Cloud
148	Build envelope, an OCI manifest, a vendor API response — keeps the foreign keys.
149	Sprued keys inside a foreign document are the tell that someone is decorating a
150	schema they don't own.
151	
152	The clause is prospective: it binds every wire format as it is authored or
153	touched; no retroactive sweep is mandated. The conversion move is catalogued as
154	ACGm_108.
155	
156	## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules
157	
158	Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:
159	
160	> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
161	> is detect-and-report, never repair.**
162	
163	Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
164	plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
165	detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.
166	
167	Three rules ride that litmus:
168	
169	1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
170	   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
171	   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
172	   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
173	   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
174	2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
175	   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
176	   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
177	   recreated.
178	3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
179	   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
180	   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
181	   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.
182	
183	## The named home — the conformance fixture
184	
185	Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
186	**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
187	(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
188	is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
189	gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.
190	
191	The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
192	`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
193	awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
194	identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
195	`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
196	proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
197	(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.
198	
199	**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population

310	  is its content.
311	- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
312	  is MCM's `mcm_tidemark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
313	  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
314	- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
315	  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
316	  narration is entangled with normative statements.
317	- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
318	  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
319	- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
320	  through git.
321	
322	### 🔧 ACGm_108: bare wire-key → minted sprue
323	
324	- **Detect:** an RB-authored JSON wire format (both sender and receiver ours)
325	  whose keys are bare terms (`name`, `digest`, `stamp`) rather than sprue-carried
326	  minted names.
327	- **Authority:** the fourth clause (wire formats are named homes). The sprue
328	  itself is governed by the mint discipline (CLAUDE.md): one sprue per format,
329	  terminal-exclusive, used for nothing else.
330	- **Licensing:** mutate-now when the format's author and every consumer convert
331	  in one move and a live verifier covers the path — a rename either resolves
332	  everywhere or the suite fails loud. Detect-only when the consumers cannot all
333	  convert together; for an RB-authored format that inability is itself a finding
334	  worth recording.
335	- **Verifier:** the consuming suite tier for the format (e.g. the capture
336	  fixtures for the Lode vouch envelope), plus the census check: `grep <sprue>_`
337	  returns exactly the format's sites and nothing else.
338	- **Done:** every key in the format carries the sprue; the format's schema
339	  identifier (if it has one) is bumped; the sprue grep is the format's complete
340	  sender/receiver census. First worked application: the Lode vouch envelope
341	  (`rblv_`, schema `rbld-vouch-2`).
342	- **Triage:** foreign-schema documents are excluded by the Palisade boundary
343	  before any sweep. Pre-MVP formats convert with no dual-dialect reading — there
344	  is no compatibility surface; standing artifacts are re-captured or discarded,
345	  never grandfathered.
346	
347	## The AXLA/MCM interface
348	
349	### The lookahead scanning rule
350	
351	When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
352	**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
353	terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
354	`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
355	distinct token type and does not shift that count.
356	
357	### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)
358	
359	ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
360	implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
361	lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
362	(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
363	`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
364	*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
365	mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
366	rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
367	dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.
368	
369	## Related Guides

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_lode_immure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation captures the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay
5	family into a single
6	{rbtga_lode}
7	— one
8	{gar_service}
9	package under the
10	{gar_lodes_namespace}
11	namespace, identified by a host-minted
12	{rbtga_touchmark}.
13	It is the podvm kind: a defensive mirror of quay's podman-machine images, which the
14	upstream rotates within days and never publishes a durable checksum for.
15	Its payload shape is opaque-blob (like the wsl kind) and its cardinality is
16	multi-member (like the reliquary kind): each captured leaf is a single-platform OCI
17	artifact — an empty config plus one `application/zstd` disk blob — riding as one
18	member tag within the one package.
19	
20	NOTE: One verb spans both quay families.
21	`immure` takes the family as its first argument — `podvm-wsl`
22	(`quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl`, consumed by Windows podman) or `podvm-native`
23	(`quay.io/podman/machine-os`, consumed by macOS/Linux podman) — and the podman
24	version as its second (e.g. `5.6`).
25	The two families diverge in version coverage and platform fan-out, so the kind-letter
26	(`vw` / `vn`), the upstream family reference, and the curated leaf set are all keyed
27	on the family argument; there is no kind-argument indirection beyond that one word.
28	
29	NOTE: Intent is declarative — no FQIN, no pinned digest.
30	The curated `{disktype × arch}` leaf set is an RB constant per family; the
31	{rbtr_director}
32	's workstation assembles no coordinate beyond the family and version, and the cloud
33	step RESOLVES each leaf digest from the live index at capture time.
34	No upstream digest lives in project config — per the no-FQIN principle, the
35	declaration stays intent and the pipeline computes the coordinate.
36	
37	NOTE: Acquisition runs cloud-side.
38	The
39	{rbtr_director}
40	submits a
41	{gcb_service}
42	job on the tethered (egress-permitted) pool, executed by the
43	{rbtr_mason}
44	service account via the metadata server.
45	The workstation never fetches the multi-gigabyte disk bytes — only the cloud step
46	copies them, registry-to-registry by digest.
47	
48	NOTE: The trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition.
49	quay publishes no durable, re-checkable checksum for podvm and rotates the images
50	out within days, so RB attests only the leaf digest observed at the moment of
51	capture — trust-on-first-acquisition, the strongest honest claim the hostile
52	upstream permits.
53	The integrity guards are `gcrane cp`-by-digest (get-or-error, digest-faithful) and a
54	registry-v2 blob-residency check, NOT a re-checkable published checksum.
55	
56	NOTE: Immure selects leaves by the index DESCRIPTOR, never the layer filename.
57	The upstream tag is a multi-arch OCI index mixing real container images and disk
58	artifacts; selection keys on each child descriptor's `platform.architecture` (the alt
59	spelling `x86_64` / `aarch64`) plus `annotations.disktype`.
60	The layer's embedded filename is unreliable for version and arch and is never read.
61	
62	NOTE: Immure bootstraps from no reliquary.
63	podvm is vessel-less — like wsl — so it has no reliquary slot to resolve tools from;
64	its index parsing rides python3 on the Google-hosted gcloud builder, and its registry
65	copy rides gcrane on the floating gcrane builder.
66	Pinning these floating builders is the bootstrap-builder digest-pin itch, not this
67	operation.
68	
69	NOTE: Immure is capture-pure.
70	It mints the Lode, emits the
71	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
72	provenance envelope, and reports the touchmark — but writes NO consumer config.
73	Consumption (a host's `podman machine init` from a captured seed, and the nameplate
74	election that pins which touchmark a node provisions) is a separate, deferred
75	consumption-side layer that reads the
76	{rbf_fact_lode}
77	this operation writes; it is not part of immure.
78	
79	The
80	{rbtr_director}
81	captures a podvm family's disk leaves into a Lode:
82	
83	//axhos_step
84	. *Authenticate as
85	{rbtr_director}*:
86	.. Execute
87	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
88	.. {rbbc_store}
89	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
90	
91	//axhos_step
92	. *Resolve the family (host-side)*:
93	.. {rbbc_store}
94	`«KIND»` (`vw` / `vn`), `«FAMILY»` (the quay family reference), and `«SELECTION»`
95	(the curated `disktype:arch` rows) from the family argument
96	.. The family-to-tuple mapping and the curated leaf set are RB constants; the version
97	is the operator's (later, a nameplate's) declarative choice
98	
99	//axhos_step
100	. *Mint Touchmark (host-side)*:
101	.. {rbbc_store}
102	`«TOUCHMARK»` as the kind-letter `«KIND»` + `YYMMDDHHMMSS` from current time
103	.. The host owns the stamp so the touchmark — and thus the
104	{rbf_fact_lode}
105	path — is known before the build
106	.. Example: `vw260610095327`
107	
108	//axhos_step
109	. *Submit Four-Step Capture Cloud Build*:
110	.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
111	... Step 1 — the Google-hosted gcloud builder, script `rbgjl07-immure-select.py` (python3: the index read + leaf select + envelope author)
112	... Step 2 — the floating gcrane builder, script `rbgjl08-immure-capture.sh` (`gcrane cp` each selected leaf by digest)
113	... Step 3 — the Google-hosted docker builder, script `rbgjl09-immure-residency.sh` (the blob-residency HEAD guard)
114	... Step 4 — the floating gcrane builder, script `rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (the vouch push)
115	... substitutions carrying GAR host/path, the
116	{gar_lodes_namespace}
117	root, `«TOUCHMARK»`, the member-tag sprue, the acquiring SA email, the recorded trust grade, the envelope schema, `«FAMILY»`, the version, and `«SELECTION»`
118	... serviceAccount:
119	{rbtr_mason}
120	... pool: tethered (the upstream index + leaf fetch requires public egress)
121	... `options.substitutionOption` ALLOW_LOOSE — the python step reads its substitutions as automapped env vars, not textual expansions, so the strict match would reject them
122	.. {rbbc_submit}
123	link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/create[builds.create] with:
124	... project:
125	{rbtgi_depot_project}
126	... location:
127	{rbrd_gcp_region}
128	.. {rbbc_store}
129	`«BUILD_ID»` from response `.metadata.build.id`
130	.. {rbbc_show}
131	build dispatched with `«BUILD_ID»` and the Cloud Console URL
132	
133	//axhos_step
134	. *Select Step (in-pool — rbgjl07, python3)*:
135	.. Acquire an anonymous registry-v2 pull token for the public quay family (Www-Authenticate discovery)
136	.. Fetch the family index at the pinned version; fail loud if it is not a multi-arch index
137	.. For each curated `disktype:arch`, select the child descriptor matching `platform.architecture` + `annotations.disktype` — failing loud, listing every available pair, when a curated entry matches nothing (so a quay rotation reads as an inventory diff)
138	.. Fetch each selected leaf manifest; assert exactly one disk-blob layer; record its digest and declared size
139	.. Author the
140	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
141	envelope JSON (schema `rbld-vouch-1`, trust grade `recorded-at-acquisition`, `members[]` one element per leaf — the cardinality axis — each carrying `verification` `recorded` and the captured leaf digest) and stage it plus the selection list on the {rbtgr_workspace}
142	.. Write the result JSON (carrying the host-minted touchmark) to `buildStepOutputs`
143	
144	//axhos_step
145	. *Capture Step (in-pool — rbgjl08, gcrane)*:
146	.. For each selection row, `gcrane cp «FAMILY»@«LEAF_DIGEST»` into the one Lode package under the clean member tag `:rbi_<disktype>-<arch>` — by digest, get-or-error, daemonless
147	.. Confirm each copied tag resolves to the selected digest (a free manifest-level readback)
148	.. Each leaf is a single-platform manifest, so the package stays FLAT — no parent-index web — keeping banish single-call atomic by construction
149	
150	//axhos_step
151	. *Residency Step (in-pool — rbgjl09)*:
152	.. For each member, HEAD the GAR registry-v2 blob and assert its `Content-Length` equals the leaf manifest's declared layer size
153	.. The anti-hollow-mirror guard the recorded grade demands — a registry can hold a manifest whose large layer never fully landed, which the digest readback alone would not catch
154	
155	//axhos_step
156	. *Vouch Step (in-pool — rbgjl02)*:
157	.. For the staged envelope, build a `FROM scratch` image copying `vouch.json` and push it to the Lode package under the `:rbi_vouch` tag — a distinct manifest sharing the one package
158	.. The recipe order is load-bearing: the vouch push runs strictly after the residency guard, so the provenance artifact never precedes the integrity check
159	
160	//axhos_step
161	. *Wait for Build Completion*:
162	.. {rbbc_poll}
163	link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.builds/get[cloudbuild.builds.get] for `«BUILD_ID»`
164	.. {rbbc_require}
165	final status is `SUCCESS`
166	
167	//axhoot_typed_output
168	{rbf_fact_lode}
169	. *Write Capture-Facts*:
170	.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` (base64-decoded JSON) from the completed build to recover the host-minted `«TOUCHMARK»`
171	.. {rbbc_store}
172	the touchmark value and the podvm kind-brand enum (`podvm-wsl` / `podvm-native`, verbatim the family argument) as the two bare single-form facts of the
173	{rbf_fact_lode}
174	— carrying no provenance content; the envelope is the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag, written cloud-side by the vouch step
175	
176	//axhoc_completion
177	Successful execution captures the selected podman-machine disk leaves into a single
178	{gar_service}
179	package at `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`, carrying each opaque disk blob under
180	its `:rbi_<disktype>-<arch>` member tag plus the `:rbi_vouch`
181	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
182	envelope, and hands the touchmark forward host-side as the
183	{rbf_fact_lode}.
184	+
185	The operation is capture-pure: no vessel or nameplate config is written.
186	The captured cohort is a Director-dictated version closure — the substrate bytes are
187	exactly the digests captured from the pinned family/version, never "whatever the
188	operator's podman happened to fetch".
189	+
190	Consumption is deferred this heat: immure stops at the registry.
191	A host's `podman machine init` from a captured seed, and the nameplate election that
192	pins which touchmark a node provisions, are a separate consumption-side layer that
193	reads the capture-file — so election retries independently and survives, and a
194	one-to-many fan-out across nodes iterates by reading the file rather than by a verb
195	taking N node arguments.
196

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_lode_augur}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation inspects a single
5	{rbtga_lode}
6	— listing its member tags and decoding its
7	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
8	provenance envelope — by querying GAR directly via the Registry API.
9	Read-only; no local container runtime.
10	Authenticates as
11	{rbtr_director}.
12	
13	NOTE: Augur is the single-Lode inspect verb — the perceiving counterpart, at member grain, to
14	{rbtgo_lode_divine}'s
15	lifecycle-grain enumeration.
16	Divine answers which Lodes exist; augur answers what is inside one — its member tags and its decoded
17	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
18	envelope.
19	The fetched-side parallel of made-side
20	{rbtgo_ark_inspect}.
21	
22	NOTE: Augur is kind-agnostic.
23	It decodes the kind from the
24	{rbtga_touchmark}
25	leading letter and reads the same-shaped envelope every kind carries — so a new Lode kind needs no change here.
26	
27	The
28	{rbtr_director}
29	augurs a Lode:
30	
31	//axhopt_typed_parameter
32	{rbtga_touchmark}
33	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
34	.. {rbbc_require}
35	`«TOUCHMARK»` provided as the operation folio
36	.. {rbbc_require}
37	`«TOUCHMARK»` matches the touchmark format `«KIND»YYMMDDHHMMSS` (see
38	{rbst_touchmark})
39	.. {rbbc_fatal}
40	if malformed: "Touchmark format invalid"
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Authenticate as
44	{rbtr_director}*:
45	.. Execute
46	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
47	.. {rbbc_store}
48	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
49	
50	//axhos_step
51	. *Fetch Member Tags*:
52	.. {rbbc_call}
53	GAR REST `tags.list` for the Lode package `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»`
54	.. {rbbc_require}
55	at least one tag present — an empty package is the "Lode not present" signal
56	.. {rbbc_store}
57	`«MEMBER_TAGS»` — every member tag on the package
58	
59	//axhos_step
60	. *Decode Provenance Envelope*:
61	.. {rbbc_call}
62	Registry API: extract the `:rbi_vouch` manifest from the `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»` package and read its `vouch.json` layer
63	.. {rbbc_store}
64	the
65	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
66	fields: `kind`, `acquired_at`, `acquired_by`, `capture_build`, `trust_grade`, and `members[]` (each `origin` / `digest` / `verification` / `tags`)
67	
68	//axhoot_typed_output
69	{rbtga_lode}
70	. *Display*:
71	.. {rbbc_show}
72	the kind (decoded from the touchmark prefix), the member tags, and the decoded provenance — origin, canonical digest, trust grade, and acquisition facts
73	.. {rbbc_show}
74	explicit trust-grade posture: `verified-against-published` versus `recorded-at-acquisition`, so the displayed claim never over-states what the upstream permits
75	
76	//axhoc_completion
77	Augur is a read-only inspection of one
78	{rbtga_lode}
79	via the GAR Registry API: it lists the member tags and decodes the
80	{rbtga_lode_vouch}
81	envelope, and modifies nothing.
82	+
83	The categorical layout makes the touchmark the primary index — every member tag and the provenance envelope sit within the one `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»` package, so no cross-Lode aggregator is needed.
84	+
85	Trust posture is reported honestly per the envelope's declared grade: a `recorded-at-acquisition` Lode states that RB attests only the digest observed at capture, never that it remains re-checkable against a vanished upstream.

18	//
19	// Cases execute inside a charged crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle).
20	// Thread-local context bridges the static case function signature with
21	// the mutable invocation context needed for tabtarget calls.

51	// ── Thread-local invocation context ──────────────────────────
52	
53	/// Ifrit binary name inside the bottle container.
54	const RBTDRC_IFRIT_BINARY: &str = "rbid";
55	
56	/// Test connectivity target — ICANN-owned, stable single /20 CIDR (192.0.32.0/20)
57	const RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN: &str = "www.internic.net";
58	
59	thread_local! {
60	    static RBTDRC_CTX: RefCell<Option<rbtdri_Context>> = RefCell::new(None);
61	}
62	
63	/// Store invocation context for case functions. Called before run_sections.
64	pub fn rbtdrc_set_context(ctx: rbtdri_Context) {
65	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| *c.borrow_mut() = Some(ctx));
66	}
67	
68	/// Retrieve invocation context after cases complete. Called for quench.
69	pub fn rbtdrc_take_context() -> rbtdri_Context {
70	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
71	        c.borrow_mut()
72	            .take()
73	            .expect("rbtdrc: no context — was rbtdrc_set_context called?")
74	    })
75	}
76	
77	pub(crate) fn rbtdrc_with_ctx<F>(f: F) -> rbtdre_Verdict
78	where
79	    F: FnOnce(&mut rbtdri_Context) -> rbtdre_Verdict,
80	{
81	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
82	        let mut opt = c.borrow_mut();
83	        let ctx = opt
84	            .as_mut()
85	            .expect("rbtdrc: no invocation context for case execution");
86	        f(ctx)
87	    })
88	}
89	
90	// ── Crucible lifecycle hooks (setup/teardown) ────────────────
91	
92	/// Setup hook for crucible fixtures: charge → CrucibleActive assertion.
93	/// Reads ctx from thread-local. Returns Err on any step failure; engine
94	/// surfaces this as a fixture failure and runs teardown anyway.
95	///
96	/// Service-readiness waits are folded into the charge tabtarget itself,
97	/// driven by the per-nameplate `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` fact.
98	pub fn rbtdrc_charge_crucible() -> Result<(), String> {
99	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
100	        let mut opt = c.borrow_mut();
101	        let ctx = opt
102	            .as_mut()
103	            .ok_or_else(|| "rbtdrc: no invocation context for charge".to_string())?;
104	        zrbtdrc_charge_impl(ctx)
105	    })
106	}
107	
108	/// Teardown hook for crucible fixtures: quench → inverse CrucibleActive
109	/// assertion. Reads ctx from thread-local. Best-effort — surfaces failures as
110	/// stderr warnings rather than errors; teardown always completes.
111	pub fn rbtdrc_quench_crucible() {
112	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
113	        let mut opt = c.borrow_mut();
114	        if let Some(ctx) = opt.as_mut() {
115	            zrbtdrc_quench_impl(ctx);
116	        } else {
117	            crate::rbtdrg_error_now!("rbtdrc: no invocation context for quench — skipped");
118	        }
119	    });
120	}
121	
122	fn zrbtdrc_charge_impl(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) -> Result<(), String> {
123	    let fixture = ctx.fixture().to_string();
124	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Charging crucible for nameplate '{}'...", fixture);
125	    match rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE, &[]) {
126	        Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => {
127	            crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Crucible charged");
128	        }
129	        Ok(r) => {
130	            return Err(format!("Charge failed (exit {})\n{}", r.exit_code, r.stderr));
131	        }
132	        Err(e) => {
133	            return Err(format!("Charge invocation failed: {}", e));
134	        }
135	    }
136	
137	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Verifying crucible is active after charge...");
138	    match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_ACTIVE, &[&fixture], &[]) {
139	        Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => {
140	            crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Crucible active confirmed");
141	        }
142	        Ok(r) => {
143	            return Err(format!(
144	                "Lifecycle assertion failed: crucible not active after charge (exit {})\n{}",
145	                r.exit_code, r.stderr
146	            ));
147	        }
148	        Err(e) => {
149	            return Err(format!(
150	                "CrucibleActive invocation failed after charge: {}",
151	                e
152	            ));
153	        }
154	    }
155	
156	    Ok(())
157	}
158	
159	fn zrbtdrc_quench_impl(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) {
160	    let fixture = ctx.fixture().to_string();
161	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Quenching crucible...");
162	    match rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH, &[]) {
163	        Ok(r) if r.exit_code == 0 => crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Crucible quenched"),
164	        Ok(r) => crate::rbtdrg_error_now!("quench exited {}", r.exit_code),
165	        Err(e) => crate::rbtdrg_error_now!("quench invocation failed: {}", e),
166	    }
167	
168	    crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Verifying crucible is inactive after quench...");
169	    match rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_ACTIVE, &[&fixture], &[]) {
170	        Ok(r) if r.exit_code != 0 => {
171	            crate::rbtdrg_info_now!("Crucible inactive confirmed");
172	        }
173	        Ok(r) => {
174	            crate::rbtdrg_error_now!(
175	                "crucible still active after quench (exit {})",
176	                r.exit_code
177	            );
178	        }
179	        Err(e) => {
180	            crate::rbtdrg_error_now!(
181	                "CrucibleActive invocation failed after quench: {}",
182	                e
183	            );
184	        }
185	    }
186	}

190	/// Invoke ifrit inside the bottle via bark, saving stdout/stderr to case dir.
191	fn rbtdrc_invoke_ifrit(
192	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
193	    attack: &str,
194	    dir: &Path,
195	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
196	    let result = match rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_BARK, &[RBTDRC_IFRIT_BINARY, attack]) {
197	        Ok(r) => r,
198	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("bark invocation error: {}", e)),
199	    };
200	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stdout.txt"), &result.stdout);
201	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stderr.txt"), &result.stderr);
202	    rbtdri_parse_ifrit_verdict(&result.stdout, result.exit_code)
203	}
204	
205	/// Invoke ifrit inside the bottle via bark with extra arguments.
206	fn rbtdrc_invoke_ifrit_with_args(
207	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
208	    attack: &str,
209	    extra_args: &[&str],
210	    dir: &Path,
211	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
212	    let mut bark_args = vec![RBTDRC_IFRIT_BINARY, attack];
213	    bark_args.extend_from_slice(extra_args);
214	    let result = match rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_BARK, &bark_args) {
215	        Ok(r) => r,
216	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("bark invocation error: {}", e)),
217	    };
218	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stdout.txt"), &result.stdout);
219	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stderr.txt"), &result.stderr);
220	    rbtdri_parse_ifrit_verdict(&result.stdout, result.exit_code)
221	}

223	/// Execute a command in the sentry via writ, returning captured stdout.
224	///
225	/// Runs under BURD_NO_LOG so the BUK dispatch does not fold the tabtarget's
226	/// stderr into stdout (nor emit log-path headers). The captured stdout is then
227	/// exactly the sentry command's output — rbob_writ's "Writ to sentry" status
228	/// line (buc_step, stderr) stays out of it, so a value-read needs no header or
229	/// ANSI filtering and is independent of terminal color (NO_COLOR, dumb TERM,
230	/// plain ssh pipe). theurge keeps its own per-invocation stdout/stderr capture,
231	/// so dropping the redundant logs-buk transcript for these probe writs costs
232	/// no diagnostics.
233	fn rbtdrc_writ(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, args: &[&str]) -> Result<String, String> {
234	    let result = rbtdri_invoke_env(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_WRIT, args, &[("BURD_NO_LOG", "1")])?;
235	    if result.exit_code != 0 {
236	        return Err(format!(
237	            "writ exit {}\nstdout: {}\nstderr: {}",
238	            result.exit_code, result.stdout, result.stderr
239	        ));
240	    }
241	    Ok(result.stdout)
242	}
243	
244	/// Execute a command in the pentacle via fiat, returning the invocation result.
245	fn rbtdrc_fiat(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, args: &[&str]) -> Result<String, String> {
246	    let result = rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_FIAT, args)?;
247	    if result.exit_code != 0 {
248	        return Err(format!(
249	            "fiat exit {}\nstdout: {}\nstderr: {}",
250	            result.exit_code, result.stdout, result.stderr
251	        ));
252	    }
253	    Ok(result.stdout)
254	}
255	
256	/// Discover the sentry's enclave IP by reading /etc/resolv.conf from the pentacle via fiat.
257	/// The pentacle uses the sentry as its DNS server, so resolv.conf nameserver = sentry enclave IP.
258	/// (Sentry's own resolv.conf points to upstream DNS like 8.8.8.8 — wrong for enclave ops.)
259	fn rbtdrc_discover_sentry_ip(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) -> Result<String, String> {
260	    let output = rbtdrc_fiat(ctx, &["cat", "/etc/resolv.conf"])?;
261	    for line in output.lines() {
262	        let trimmed = line.trim();
263	        if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix("nameserver") {
264	            let ip = rest.trim();
265	            if !ip.is_empty() && rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(ip) {
266	                return Ok(ip.to_string());
267	            }
268	        }
269	    }
270	    Err(format!(
271	        "no nameserver found in pentacle /etc/resolv.conf:\n{}",
272	        output
273	    ))
274	}
275	
276	/// Resolve a hostname via writ (running dig +short on the sentry).
277	/// Returns the first line that looks like an IP address (filters BUK log headers).
278	fn rbtdrc_resolve_via_writ(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, hostname: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
279	    let output = rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["dig", "+short", hostname])?;
280	    let ip = output
281	        .lines()
282	        .find(|line| {
283	            let t = line.trim();
284	            !t.is_empty() && rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(t)
285	        })
286	        .map(|line| line.trim().to_string())
287	        .ok_or_else(|| format!("dig +short {} returned no IP:\n{}", hostname, output))?;
288	    Ok(ip)
289	}
290	
291	/// Quick check: does this string look like an IPv4 address (digits and dots only)?
292	/// Not a full validator — just enough to reject BUK log headers and DNS comments.
293	fn rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(s: &str) -> bool {
294	    !s.is_empty() && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit() || c == '.')
295	}
296	
297	/// Extract iptables rule lines from writ output, filtering BUK log headers.
298	/// Rules start with -P (policy), -N (new chain), or -A (append).
299	fn rbtdrc_extract_iptables_rules(output: &str) -> String {
300	    output
301	        .lines()
302	        .filter(|l| {
303	            let t = l.trim();
304	            t.starts_with("-P ") || t.starts_with("-N ") || t.starts_with("-A ")
305	        })
306	        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
307	        .join("\n")
308	}
309	
310	/// Read a named environment variable from sentry's process environment via writ.
311	/// writ runs under BURD_NO_LOG, so its stdout is the clean command output —
312	/// the first non-empty line is the value, with no header/ANSI stripping needed.
313	fn rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, var: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
314	    let output = rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["printenv", var])?;
315	    output
316	        .lines()
317	        .map(|l| l.trim())
318	        .find(|l| !l.is_empty())
319	        .map(|l| l.to_string())
320	        .ok_or_else(|| format!("printenv {} returned no value", var))
321	}

323	// ── Basic infra cases (fiat) ──────────────────────────────────
324	
325	fn rbtdrc_pentacle_dnsmasq_responds(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
326	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
327	        let sentry_ip = match rbtdrc_discover_sentry_ip(ctx) {
328	            Ok(ip) => ip,
329	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("sentry IP discovery: {}", e)),
330	        };
331	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("sentry-ip.txt"), &sentry_ip);
332	
333	        let output = match rbtdrc_fiat(
334	            ctx,
335	            &["dig", "+short", &format!("@{}", sentry_ip), RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN],
336	        ) {
337	            Ok(o) => o,
338	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("fiat dig error: {}", e)),
339	        };
340	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("fiat-stdout.txt"), &output);
341	
342	        if output.trim().is_empty() {
343	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
344	                "dnsmasq on sentry {} returned empty response for {}",
345	                sentry_ip, RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN
346	            ));
347	        }
348	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
349	    })
350	}

353	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {

356	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("sentry IP discovery: {}", e)),

358	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("sentry-ip.txt"), &sentry_ip);

360	        let output = match rbtdrc_fiat(ctx, &["ping", &sentry_ip, "-c", "2"]) {

363	        };

366	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass

438	fn rbtdrc_sentry_iptables_loaded(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
439	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
440	        let output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-S"]) {
441	            Ok(o) => o,
442	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("writ error: {}", e)),
443	        };
444	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("iptables-rules.txt"), &output);
445	
446	        if output.trim().is_empty() {
447	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("iptables -S returned empty output".to_string());
448	        }
449	        // Expect at least one policy line (-P) and one append rule (-A)
450	        if !output.contains("-P") || !output.contains("-A") {
451	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
452	                "iptables rules incomplete (missing -P or -A):\n{}",
453	                output
454	            ));
455	        }
456	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
457	    })
458	}

460	// Structural-presence backstops for the load-bearing iptables clauses and
461	// kernel sysctl set by rbjs_sentry.sh. Each case is a fast read-and-check
462	// against sentry's runtime state — fail loudly if a future change strips a
463	// defensive clause the architecture depends on.
464	
465	fn rbtdrc_sentry_config_rp_filter(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
466	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
467	        let entry_mode = match rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx, "RBRN_ENTRY_MODE") {
468	            Ok(m) => m,
469	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read RBRN_ENTRY_MODE: {}", e)),
470	        };
471	        let output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["cat", "/proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter"]) {
472	            Ok(o) => o,
473	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("writ error: {}", e)),
474	        };
475	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("rp_filter.txt"), &output);
476	
477	        let value = output
478	            .lines()
479	            .map(|l| l.trim())
480	            .find(|l| !l.is_empty())
481	            .map(|l| l.to_string())
482	            .unwrap_or_default();
483	
484	        let expected = match entry_mode.as_str() {
485	            "rbnne_enabled" => "2",
486	            "rbnne_disabled" => "1",
487	            other => {
488	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("unexpected RBRN_ENTRY_MODE '{}'", other));
489	            }
490	        };
491	
492	        if value != expected {
493	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
494	                "rp_filter = {} (expected {} for entry-mode {})",
495	                value, expected, entry_mode
496	            ));
497	        }
498	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
499	    })
500	}

502	fn rbtdrc_sentry_config_prerouting_dnat(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
503	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
504	        let entry_mode = match rbtdrc_read_sentry_env(ctx, "RBRN_ENTRY_MODE") {
505	            Ok(m) => m,
506	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read RBRN_ENTRY_MODE: {}", e)),
507	        };
508	        if entry_mode != "rbnne_enabled" {
509	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Skip(format!("entry-mode={}", entry_mode));
510	        }
511	        let output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-t", "nat", "-S", "PREROUTING"]) {
512	            Ok(o) => o,
513	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("writ error: {}", e)),
514	        };
515	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("prerouting.txt"), &output);
516	
517	        let append_rules: Vec<&str> = output
518	            .lines()
519	            .filter(|l| l.trim().starts_with("-A "))
520	            .collect();
521	
522	        let return_positions: Vec<usize> = append_rules
523	            .iter()
524	            .enumerate()
525	            .filter(|(_, r)| r.contains("-j RETURN"))
526	            .map(|(i, _)| i)
527	            .collect();
528	        let dnat_positions: Vec<usize> = append_rules
529	            .iter()
530	            .enumerate()
531	            .filter(|(_, r)| r.contains("-j DNAT"))
532	            .map(|(i, _)| i)
533	            .collect();
534	
535	        if return_positions.len() != 2 {
536	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
537	                "PREROUTING RETURN rule count = {} (expected 2)",
538	                return_positions.len()
539	            ));
540	        }
541	        if dnat_positions.is_empty() {
542	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("PREROUTING DNAT rule absent".to_string());
543	        }
544	        let max_return = *return_positions.iter().max().unwrap();
545	        let min_dnat = *dnat_positions.iter().min().unwrap();
546	        if max_return >= min_dnat {
547	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
548	                "PREROUTING RETURN at position {} not before DNAT at position {}",
549	                max_return, min_dnat
550	            ));
551	        }
552	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
553	    })
554	}

556	fn rbtdrc_sentry_config_postrouting_masquerade(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {

572	        // (uplink path, set when uplink access-mode != disabled) has neither.

579	                "POSTROUTING entry-port MASQUERADE rule absent".to_string(),
580	            );
581	        }
582	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
583	    })
584	}

586	fn rbtdrc_sentry_config_forward_estab_related(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
587	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
588	        let output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-S", "FORWARD"]) {
589	            Ok(o) => o,
590	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("writ error: {}", e)),
591	        };
592	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("forward.txt"), &output);
593	
594	        let append_rules: Vec<&str> = output
595	            .lines()
596	            .filter(|l| l.trim().starts_with("-A "))
597	            .collect();
598	
599	        let estab_pos = append_rules
600	            .iter()
601	            .position(|r| r.contains("RELATED") && r.contains("ESTABLISHED") && r.contains("-j ACCEPT"));
602	        let jump_pos = append_rules
603	            .iter()
604	            .position(|r| r.contains("-j RBM-FORWARD"));
605	
606	        let estab_pos = match estab_pos {
607	            Some(p) => p,
608	            None => {
609	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
610	                    "FORWARD ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT rule absent".to_string(),
611	                );
612	            }
613	        };
614	        let jump_pos = match jump_pos {
615	            Some(p) => p,
616	            None => {
617	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("FORWARD -j RBM-FORWARD jump absent".to_string());
618	            }
619	        };
620	
621	        if estab_pos >= jump_pos {
622	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
623	                "FORWARD ESTABLISHED,RELATED at position {} not before RBM-FORWARD jump at position {}",
624	                estab_pos, jump_pos
625	            ));
626	        }
627	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
628	    })
629	}

631	fn rbtdrc_dns_blocked_with_observation(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
632	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
633	        // Before: capture iptables state from sentry
634	        let before = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-L", "-v", "-n", "-x"]) {
635	            Ok(o) => {
636	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("iptables-before.txt"), &o);
637	                o
638	            }
639	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("before-capture: {}", e)),
640	        };
641	
642	        // Attack: invoke ifrit dns-blocked-google inside bottle
643	        let ifrit_verdict = rbtdrc_invoke_ifrit(ctx, "dns-blocked-google", dir);
644	
645	        // After: capture iptables state from sentry
646	        let after = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["iptables", "-L", "-v", "-n", "-x"]) {
647	            Ok(o) => {
648	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("iptables-after.txt"), &o);
649	                o
650	            }
651	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("after-capture: {}", e)),
652	        };
653	
654	        // Log observation: did sentry state change during the attack?
655	        let delta = if before != after {
656	            "CHANGED — sentry processed traffic during attack"
657	        } else {
658	            "UNCHANGED — no observable sentry state change"
659	        };
660	        let observation = format!(
661	            "Ifrit verdict: {}\nIptables delta: {}\n",
662	            match &ifrit_verdict {
663	                rbtdre_Verdict::Pass => "PASS",
664	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(_) => "FAIL",
665	                rbtdre_Verdict::Skip(_) => "SKIP",
666	            },
667	            delta,
668	        );
669	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("observation.txt"), &observation);
670	
671	        // Primary verdict is the ifrit result
672	        ifrit_verdict
673	    })
674	}

816	
817	/// Coordinated: attempt outbound connections from sentry itself to non-allowed destinations.
818	/// The sentry's OUTPUT DROP policy should block these — verifies sentry can't be used as a pivot.
819	fn rbtdrc_coordinated_sentry_egress_lockdown(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
820	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
821	        // Negative: attempt TCP to 1.1.1.1:443 from sentry via bash /dev/tcp
822	        let tcp1_result = rbtdri_invoke(
823	            ctx,
824	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_WRIT,
825	            &["timeout", "2", "bash", "-c", "echo > /dev/tcp/1.1.1.1/443"],
826	        );
827	        let tcp1_blocked = match &tcp1_result {
828	            Ok(r) => {
829	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp1-stdout.txt"), &r.stdout);
830	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp1-stderr.txt"), &r.stderr);
831	                r.exit_code != 0
832	            }
833	            Err(e) => {
834	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp1-error.txt"), format!("{}", e));
835	                true // invocation error = blocked
836	            }
837	        };
838	        if !tcp1_blocked {
839	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
840	                "BREACH: sentry TCP to 1.1.1.1:443 succeeded — OUTPUT chain not blocking egress"
841	                    .to_string(),
842	            );
843	        }
844	
845	        // Negative: attempt TCP to 140.82.121.4:443 (GitHub) from sentry
846	        let tcp2_result = rbtdri_invoke(
847	            ctx,
848	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_WRIT,
849	            &[
850	                "timeout",
851	                "2",
852	                "bash",
853	                "-c",
854	                "echo > /dev/tcp/140.82.121.4/443",
855	            ],
856	        );
857	        let tcp2_blocked = match &tcp2_result {
858	            Ok(r) => {
859	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp2-stdout.txt"), &r.stdout);
860	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp2-stderr.txt"), &r.stderr);
861	                r.exit_code != 0
862	            }
863	            Err(e) => {
864	                let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("tcp2-error.txt"), format!("{}", e));
865	                true
866	            }
867	        };
868	        if !tcp2_blocked {
869	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
870	                "BREACH: sentry TCP to 140.82.121.4:443 succeeded — OUTPUT chain not blocking egress"
871	                    .to_string(),
872	            );
873	        }
874	
875	        // Positive control: dig @8.8.8.8 connectivity domain from sentry must succeed
876	        // (proves dnsmasq's egress path works — specific OUTPUT ACCEPT for DNS)
877	        let dig_result = match rbtdrc_writ(
878	            ctx,
879	            &["dig", "+short", "@8.8.8.8", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN],
880	        ) {
881	            Ok(o) => o,
882	            Err(e) => {
883	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
884	                    "positive control failed: dig @8.8.8.8 {} from sentry: {}", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN,
885	                    e
886	                ))
887	            }
888	        };
889	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("dig-positive.txt"), &dig_result);
890	
891	        let has_ip = dig_result
892	            .lines()
893	            .any(|l| rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(l.trim()));
894	        if !has_ip {
895	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
896	                "positive control: dig @8.8.8.8 {} returned no IP:\n{}", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN,
897	                dig_result
898	            ));
899	        }
900	
901	        let _ = std::fs::write(
902	            dir.join("observation.txt"),
903	            "Sentry egress lockdown verified: TCP to 1.1.1.1:443 blocked, TCP to 140.82.121.4:443 blocked, DNS egress works",
904	        );
905	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
906	    })
907	}
