909	/// Coordinated: invoke ifrit DNS queries (allowed + blocked), then read dnsmasq log
910	/// to verify both queries appear. Proves audit trail is intact.
911	fn rbtdrc_coordinated_dnsmasq_query_audit(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
912	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
913	        // Invoke ifrit dns-allowed-example via bark
914	        let allowed_result = match rbtdri_invoke(
915	            ctx,
916	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_BARK,
917	            &[RBTDRC_IFRIT_BINARY, "dns-allowed-example"],
918	        ) {
919	            Ok(r) => r,
920	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("bark dns-allowed-example: {}", e)),
921	        };
922	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("allowed-stdout.txt"), &allowed_result.stdout);
923	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("allowed-stderr.txt"), &allowed_result.stderr);
924	
925	        // Invoke ifrit dns-blocked-google via bark
926	        let blocked_result = match rbtdri_invoke(
927	            ctx,
928	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_BARK,
929	            &[RBTDRC_IFRIT_BINARY, "dns-blocked-google"],
930	        ) {
931	            Ok(r) => r,
932	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("bark dns-blocked-google: {}", e)),
933	        };
934	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("blocked-stdout.txt"), &blocked_result.stdout);
935	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("blocked-stderr.txt"), &blocked_result.stderr);
936	
937	        // Brief delay for dnsmasq log flush
938	        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));
939	
940	        // Read dnsmasq log from sentry via writ
941	        let log_output = match rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["cat", "/var/log/dnsmasq.log"]) {
942	            Ok(o) => o,
943	            Err(e) => {
944	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
945	                    "cannot read dnsmasq log: {}",
946	                    e
947	                ))
948	            }
949	        };
950	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("dnsmasq-log.txt"), &log_output);
951	
952	        // writ runs under BURD_NO_LOG, so log_output is the raw dnsmasq log.
953	        let log_content = &log_output;
954	
955	        // Verify connectivity domain query appears in log
956	        let has_example = log_content
957	            .lines()
958	            .any(|l| l.contains(RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN));
959	        if !has_example {
960	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
961	                format!("dnsmasq log missing {} query — audit trail incomplete", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN),
962	            );
963	        }
964	
965	        // Verify google.com query appears in log (blocked queries should still be logged)
966	        let has_google = log_content
967	            .lines()
968	            .any(|l| l.contains("google.com"));
969	        if !has_google {
970	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
971	                "dnsmasq log missing google.com query — blocked queries not audited".to_string(),
972	            );
973	        }
974	
975	        let _ = std::fs::write(
976	            dir.join("observation.txt"),
977	            format!("dnsmasq audit verified: both allowed ({}) and blocked (google.com) queries logged", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN),
978	        );
979	        rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
980	    })
981	}
982	
983	/// Coordinated: ifrit sends TCP RST packets at sentry DNS, theurge verifies DNS still works.
984	fn rbtdrc_coordinated_tcp_rst_hijack(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
985	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
986	        let sentry_ip = match rbtdrc_discover_sentry_ip(ctx) {
987	            Ok(ip) => ip,
988	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("sentry IP discovery: {}", e)),
989	        };
990	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("sentry-ip.txt"), &sentry_ip);
991	        let dig_server = format!("@{}", sentry_ip);
992	
993	        // Pre-snapshot: verify DNS works on sentry before attack
994	        let pre_resolve = match rbtdrc_writ(
995	            ctx,
996	            &["dig", "+short", &dig_server, RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN],
997	        ) {
998	            Ok(o) => o,
999	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("pre dig {}: {}", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN, e)),
1000	        };
1001	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("pre-resolve.txt"), &pre_resolve);
1002	
1003	        let pre_ip = pre_resolve
1004	            .lines()
1005	            .find(|l| rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(l.trim()))
1006	            .unwrap_or("")
1007	            .trim()
1008	            .to_string();
1009	        if pre_ip.is_empty() {
1010	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
1011	                format!("pre-snapshot: {} did not resolve — DNS not working before attack", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN),
1012	            );
1013	        }
1014	
1015	        // Attack: invoke tcp-rst-hijack from bottle
1016	        let result = match rbtdri_invoke(
1017	            ctx,
1018	            RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_BARK,
1019	            &[RBTDRC_IFRIT_BINARY, "tcp-rst-hijack"],
1020	        ) {
1021	            Ok(r) => r,
1022	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("bark invocation: {}", e)),
1023	        };
1024	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stdout.txt"), &result.stdout);
1025	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("bark-stderr.txt"), &result.stderr);
1026	
1027	        // Brief pause for any RST effects to propagate
1028	        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));
1029	
1030	        // Post-snapshot: verify DNS still works on sentry after attack
1031	        let post_resolve = match rbtdrc_writ(
1032	            ctx,
1033	            &["dig", "+short", &dig_server, RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN],
1034	        ) {
1035	            Ok(o) => o,
1036	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("post dig {}: {}", RBTDRC_CONNECTIVITY_DOMAIN, e)),
1037	        };
1038	        let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("post-resolve.txt"), &post_resolve);
1039	
1040	        let post_ip = post_resolve
1041	            .lines()
1042	            .find(|l| rbtdrc_looks_like_ip(l.trim()))
1043	            .unwrap_or("")
1044	            .trim()
1045	            .to_string();

80	// Repo-root-relative kit paths. Every probate harness runs with cwd = repo
81	// root, so these are repo-root-relative. Hoisted per RCG Identity Rule (cf.
82	// VVCC_REGISTRY_PATH) so running-code joins reference a named const rather
83	// than an inline magic string.
84	const RBTDRF_BUK_ROOT: &str = "Tools/buk";
85	const RBTDRF_RBK_ROOT: &str = "Tools/rbk";
86	const RBTDRF_BUV_VALIDATION: &str = "Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh";
87	const RBTDRF_BUC_COMMAND: &str = "Tools/buk/buc_command.sh";
88	const RBTDRF_RBLDS_SPINE: &str = "Tools/rbk/rblds_Spine.sh";

90	// ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
91	
92	/// Sub-assertion within a case: run bash snippet, check exit code.
93	struct RbtdrfSub {
94	    label: &'static str,
95	    setup: &'static str,
96	    command: &'static str,
97	    expect_ok: bool,
98	}
99	
100	impl RbtdrfSub {
101	    const fn ok(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
102	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: true }
103	    }
104	    const fn fatal(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
105	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: false }
106	    }
107	    const fn ok_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
108	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: true }
109	    }
110	    const fn fatal_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
111	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: false }
112	    }
113	}
114	
115	/// Run a bash script, return (exit_code, stdout, stderr). Saves traces to case dir.
116	fn rbtdrf_run_bash(
117	    project_root: &Path,
118	    script: &str,
119	    dir: &Path,
120	    trace_prefix: &str,
121	) -> Result<(i32, String, String), String> {
122	    let output = Command::new(rbtdri_bash_program())
123	        .arg("-c")
124	        .arg(script)
125	        .current_dir(project_root)
126	        .output()
127	        .map_err(|e| format!("bash execution failed: {}", e))?;
128	
129	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).into_owned();
130	    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned();
131	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
132	
133	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-script.sh", trace_prefix)), script);
134	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", trace_prefix)), &stdout);
135	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", trace_prefix)), &stderr);
136	
137	    Ok((code, stdout, stderr))
138	}
139	
140	/// Run enrollment-validation sub-assertions against BUV.
141	fn rbtdrf_run_ev(
142	    dir: &Path,
143	    enrollment: &str,
144	    subs: &[RbtdrfSub],
145	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
146	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
147	        Ok(r) => r,
148	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
149	    };
150	    let buv = root.join(RBTDRF_BUV_VALIDATION);
151	
152	    for (i, sub) in subs.iter().enumerate() {
153	        let script = format!(
154	            "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n{}\n{}\n{}",
155	            rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
156	            enrollment,
157	            sub.setup,
158	            sub.command,
159	        );
160	
161	        match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, &format!("sub-{}", i)) {
162	            Ok((code, _, _)) => {
163	                let ok = code == 0;
164	                if ok != sub.expect_ok {
165	                    return if sub.expect_ok {
166	                        rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", sub.label, code))
167	                    } else {
168	                        rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected failure, got exit 0", sub.label))
169	                    };
170	                }
171	            }
172	            Err(e) => {
173	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", sub.label, e));
174	            }
175	        }
176	    }
177	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
178	}

180	/// Drive a regime's public `*_probate` function against a synthetic
181	/// baseline+override written to the case temp dir. `expect_ok` selects the
182	/// verdict polarity: pass-anchors validate the pristine baseline (true);
183	/// negatives apply one violating override and expect non-zero (false).
184	/// Sources only the contract surface — `buv_validation.sh` plus the one regime
185	/// module — mirroring the `buv_vet` bridge that enrollment-validation uses: no
186	/// internal-module chain, no z-private reach.
187	///
188	/// `tools_subdir` is the repo-relative directory holding the module
189	/// ("Tools/rbk" or "Tools/buk"). `prelude` is raw bash staged after buv's
190	/// kindle but before the regime module's probate — for regimes whose kindle or
191	/// enforce reaches a neighbor (rbrp → rbgc's payor regex; burs → bubc, whose
192	/// constants its enrollment help-string expands). Relative paths resolve
193	/// against cwd (the repo root, set by rbtdrf_run_bash). Empty for the common
194	/// case, mirroring how rbtdrf_rs_rbrr_nonempty_prefix stages rbgc in-harness.
195	fn rbtdrf_run_probate_in(
196	    dir: &Path,
197	    tools_subdir: &str,
198	    module: &str,
199	    probate_fn: &str,
200	    prelude: &str,
201	    baseline: &str,
202	    override_: &str,
203	    expect_ok: bool,
204	    label: &str,
205	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
206	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
207	        Ok(r) => r,
208	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
209	    };
210	    let buv = root.join(RBTDRF_BUV_VALIDATION);
211	    let module_path = root.join(tools_subdir).join(module);
212	
213	    let env_file = dir.join(format!("{}.env", label));
214	    let body = format!("{}\n{}\n", baseline, override_);
215	    if let Err(e) = std::fs::write(&env_file, &body) {
216	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: write env failed: {}", label, e));
217	    }
218	
219	    let script = format!(
220	        "set -euo pipefail\n\
221	         source '{}'\n\
222	         source '{}'\n\
223	         zbuv_kindle\n\
224	         {}\
225	         {} '{}'",
226	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
227	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&module_path),
228	        prelude,
229	        probate_fn,
230	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&env_file),
231	    );
232	
233	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, label) {
234	        Ok((code, _, _)) => {
235	            let ok = code == 0;
236	            if ok == expect_ok {
237	                rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
238	            } else if expect_ok {
239	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", label, code))
240	            } else {
241	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected failure, got exit 0", label))
242	            }
243	        }
244	        Err(e) => rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", label, e)),
245	    }
246	}

248	/// Common-case wrapper: RBK module, no prereqs. Preserves the call shape used
249	/// by the reference rv_ quartet (rbrr/rbrd/rbrv/rbrn).
250	fn rbtdrf_run_probate(
251	    dir: &Path,
252	    module: &str,
253	    probate_fn: &str,
254	    baseline: &str,
255	    override_: &str,
256	    expect_ok: bool,
257	    label: &str,
258	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
259	    rbtdrf_run_probate_in(

261	    )
262	}

264	/// Run a tabtarget and check exit 0.
265	fn rbtdrf_run_tt(
266	    project_root: &Path,
267	    colophon: &str,
268	    args: &[&str],
269	    dir: &Path,
270	    label: &str,
271	) -> Result<(), String> {
272	    let tt = rbtdri_find_tabtarget_global(project_root, colophon)?;
273	    let output = rbtdri_tabtarget_command(&tt)
274	        .args(args)
275	        .current_dir(project_root)
276	        .output()
277	        .map_err(|e| format!("{}: failed to run {}: {}", label, tt.display(), e))?;
278	
279	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
280	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", label)), &output.stdout);
281	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", label)), &output.stderr);
282	
283	    if code != 0 {
284	        return Err(format!(
285	            "{}: {} exited {} — {}",
286	            label,
287	            colophon,
288	            code,
289	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr),
290	        ));
291	    }
292	    Ok(())
293	}
294	
295	/// Run a tabtarget that is expected to fail. Inverts rbtdrf_run_tt:
296	/// non-zero exit returns Ok(()), zero exit returns Err. Invocation errors
297	/// (tabtarget not found, launcher failure) still propagate as Err.
298	fn rbtdrf_run_tt_neg(
299	    project_root: &Path,
300	    colophon: &str,
301	    args: &[&str],
302	    dir: &Path,
303	    label: &str,
304	) -> Result<(), String> {
305	    let tt = rbtdri_find_tabtarget_global(project_root, colophon)?;
306	    let output = rbtdri_tabtarget_command(&tt)
307	        .args(args)
308	        .current_dir(project_root)
309	        .output()
310	        .map_err(|e| format!("{}: failed to run {}: {}", label, tt.display(), e))?;
311	
312	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
313	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", label)), &output.stdout);
314	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", label)), &output.stderr);
315	
316	    if code == 0 {
317	        return Err(format!(
318	            "{}: {} expected failure, got exit 0",
319	            label, colophon,
320	        ));
321	    }
322	    Ok(())
323	}

579	// --- Reference types ---
580	
581	const RBTDRF_VALID_DIGEST: &str =
582	    "sha256:abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789";
583	
584	fn rbtdrf_ev_odref_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
585	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
586	        Ok(r) => r,
587	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
588	    };
589	    let buv = root.join(RBTDRF_BUV_VALIDATION);
590	    let enrollment = format!(
591	        "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n\
592	         buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"References\"\n\
593	         buv_odref_enroll TEST_IMAGE \"Container image\"",
594	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
595	    );
596	    let d = RBTDRF_VALID_DIGEST;
597	
598	    let subs: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
599	        ("standard registry", &format!("docker.io/library/alpine@{}", d)),
600	        ("multi-level repo", &format!("us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-proj/my-repo/tool@{}", d)),
601	        ("registry with port", &format!("registry.local:5000/myimage@{}", d)),
602	    ];
603	
604	    for (i, (label, image)) in subs.iter().enumerate() {
605	        let script = format!("{}\nexport TEST_IMAGE=\"{}\"\nbuv_vet \"TEST\"", enrollment, image);
606	        match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, &format!("sub-{}", i)) {
607	            Ok((0, _, _)) => {}
608	            Ok((code, _, _)) => {
609	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", label, code));
610	            }
611	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", label, e)),
612	        }
613	    }
614	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
615	}
616

620	         buv_odref_enroll TEST_IMAGE \"Container image\"",
621	        &[RbtdrfSub::fatal("tag only",
622	            "export TEST_IMAGE=\"docker.io/library/alpine:latest\"")],
623	    )
624	}

847	fn rbtdrf_ev_multiscope(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
848	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
849	        Ok(r) => r,
850	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
851	    };
852	    let buv = root.join(RBTDRF_BUV_VALIDATION);
853	
854	    // Two-scope enrollment: ALPHA and BETA
855	    let enrollment = format!(
856	        "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n\
857	         buv_regime_enroll \"ALPHA\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Alpha Vars\"\n\
858	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_ALPHA_FLAG \"Alpha flag\"\n\
859	         buv_regime_enroll \"BETA\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Beta Vars\"\n\
860	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_BETA_FLAG \"Beta flag\"\n\
861	         export TEST_ALPHA_FLAG=\"1\"\nexport TEST_BETA_FLAG=\"bad\"\n",
862	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
863	    );
864	
865	    // Sub 1: vet ALPHA passes (BETA is bad but not in scope)
866	    let script1 = format!("{}buv_vet \"ALPHA\"", enrollment);
867	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script1, dir, "sub-0") {
868	        Ok((code, _, _)) if code == 0 => {}
869	        Ok((code, _, _)) => {
870	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("alpha scope: expected ok, got exit {}", code));
871	        }
872	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("alpha scope: {}", e)),
873	    }
874	
875	    // Sub 2: vet BETA fails
876	    let script2 = format!("{}buv_vet \"BETA\"", enrollment);
877	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script2, dir, "sub-1") {
878	        Ok((code, _, _)) if code != 0 => {}
879	        Ok((_, _, _)) => {
880	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("beta scope: expected failure, got exit 0".to_string());
881	        }
882	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("beta scope: {}", e)),
883	    }
884	
885	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
886	}

117	# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed underpin build and
118	# emit the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand
119	# enum). The fetch step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the
120	# host-minted stamp in slot_1; the wrap and vouch-push steps write no output. Underpin
121	# captures exactly one Lode, so exactly one slot is populated. The provenance
122	# envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR (rbgjl02 pushed it under
123	# :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the touchmark a consumer needs.
124	zrbld_underpin_extract() {
125	  zrbld_sentinel
126	
127	  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"
128	
129	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_UNDERPIN_PREFIX}output.json"
130	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
131	
132	  buc_log_args "Underpin output:"
133	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"
134	
135	  local -r z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_UNDERPIN_PREFIX}stamp.txt"
136	  jq -r '.slot_1.stamp // empty' "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
137	    || buc_die "Failed to read wsl stamp from underpin output"
138	  local -r z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
139	  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Underpin output carried no stamp in slot_1"
140	
141	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
142	    || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
143	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_WSL}" \
144	    || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
145	  buc_success "Underpin captured Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_WSL})"
146	}

content.text: Now update the native selection constant (TASK 3). The memo §5 says the full native curated leaf set comes from the native family's 8 `disktype` children: `{aarch64, x86_64} × {applehv, hyperv, qemu, wsl}`:

input.old_string:
# Curated leaf selection per family — declarative `disktype:arch` rows the select
  # step matches against index child descriptors. THIS PACE proves the podvm-wsl
  # family end-to-end (both wsl-disktype leaves -> a genuine 2-member Lode, which
  # exercises the multi-member machinery and the per-member-jettison path). The
  # native family's full 8-leaf curation and a same-version "widen the set" refresh
  # mode are the FOLLOWING pace (RBSL "Why podvm kinds retain selectively"); the
  # native default below is the modest 2-leaf set the cerebro experiment captured
  # (memo-20260608 §5) — the verb works for native, but only podvm-wsl is fixture-
  # proven. Member tag composes as :<sprue><disktype>-<arch> (e.g. rbi_wsl-x86_64);
  # disktype+arch are RB-selected from the index, so sprued.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_WSL_SELECTION="wsl:x86_64 wsl:aarch64"
  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_NATIVE_SELECTION="qemu:x86_64 wsl:x86_64"
input.new_string:
# Curated leaf selection per family — declarative `disktype:arch` rows the select
  # step matches against index child descriptors (alt arch spelling: x86_64/aarch64).
  # Member tag composes as :<sprue><disktype>-<arch> (e.g. rbi_wsl-x86_64);
  # disktype+arch are RB-selected from the index, so sprued.
  # WSL family: 2-leaf set (both wsl-disktype leaves). The podvm-wsl fixture proves
  # this end-to-end with the multi-member machinery and per-member-jettison path.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_WSL_SELECTION="wsl:x86_64 wsl:aarch64"
  # Native family: full 8-leaf curation — {applehv, hyperv, qemu, wsl} × {x86_64, aarch64}.
  # Sourced from memo-20260608 §5 (the machine-os index carries exactly these 8 disktype
  # children at the 5.6 observation point; the 2 plain-container children are EXCLUDED
  # by disktype-key selection). This pace (lode-podvm-platform-fanout) lands full
  # curation; the "FOLLOWING pace" deferral is retired. Native full-curation is gated
  # one-time (not a recurring service fixture) — see the podvm-lifecycle fixture comment.
  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_NATIVE_SELECTION="applehv:x86_64 applehv:aarch64 hyperv:x86_64 hyperv:aarch64 qemu:x86_64 qemu:aarch64 wsl:x86_64 wsl:aarch64"

content.text: Now let's update rbld_immure to support the --refresh mode (TASK 4). First, update the external function:

input.old_string:
rbld_immure() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Wall in the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay family into a Lode (podvm kind, rbi_ld capture)"
  buc_doc_param "family"  "Quay family — ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE}"
  buc_doc_param "version" "Podman version tag on the family index (e.g. 5.6)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Two declarative arguments (no FQIN — see RBSLI): the param1 channel routes the
  # first to BUZ_FOLIO and forwards the rest, so family is the folio and version the
  # first positional. The curated leaf-set is a per-family constant; the cloud step
  # resolves leaf digests from the live index at capture time.
  local -r z_brand="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_version="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_brand}"   || buc_die "family argument required (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE})"
  test -n "${z_version}" || buc_die "version argument required (e.g. 5.6)"

  local z_kind="" z_quay_family="" z_selection=""
  zrbld_immure_resolve_family "${z_brand}"
  buc_info "Immure family: ${z_brand} (${z_quay_family}:${z_version}), leaves: ${z_selection}"

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns the
  # stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file. podvm
  # kind-letters are two characters (vw/vn); the augur touchmark regex accepts the
  # multi-letter prefix.
  local -r z_stamp="${z_kind}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"

  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_immure_submit "${z_token}" "${z_brand}" "${z_quay_family}" "${z_version}" "${z_selection}" "${z_stamp}"
  zrbld_immure_extract "${z_brand}"

  buc_success "Immure complete: ${z_quay_family}:${z_version} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}
input.new_string:
rbld_immure() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Wall in the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay family into a Lode (podvm kind, rbi_ld capture)"
  buc_doc_param "family"          "Quay family — ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE}"
  buc_doc_param "version|--refresh <touchmark>" \
    "Fresh: version tag (e.g. 5.6); Refresh: literal '--refresh' followed by existing touchmark"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # BUZ_FOLIO carries the family argument (param1 channel). $1 is either the
  # version (fresh) or the literal '--refresh' (refresh mode) with $2 as the
  # existing touchmark. Refresh reuses the same stamp (no version bump possible);
  # the locked version is derived from any member's rblv_origin in the existing envelope.
  local -r z_brand="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_brand}" || buc_die "family argument required (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL} or ${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE})"

  local z_mode="fresh"
  local z_version=""
  local z_stamp=""

  if [ "${1:-}" = "--refresh" ]; then
    z_mode="refresh"
    local -r z_touchmark="${2:-}"
    test -n "${z_touchmark}" || buc_die "touchmark required after --refresh (e.g. vw260610095327)"
    z_stamp="${z_touchmark}"
    buc_info "Immure REFRESH mode: reusing existing Lode ${z_touchmark}"
  else
    z_version="${1:-}"
    test -n "${z_version}" || buc_die "version argument required (e.g. 5.6), or use --refresh <touchmark> for refresh mode"
  fi

  local z_kind="" z_quay_family="" z_selection=""
  zrbld_immure_resolve_family "${z_brand}"

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Fresh mode: mint a new stamp and compute the preserved substitution (empty — no
  # prior envelope exists). Refresh mode: reuse the existing stamp and compute the
  # present-set from the existing GAR envelope + member tags.
  local z_preserved_members="[]"
  if [ "${z_mode}" = "fresh" ]; then
    # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
    # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
    # podvm kind-letters are two characters (vw/vn).
    z_stamp="${z_kind}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
    buc_info "Immure FRESH: ${z_brand} (${z_quay_family}:${z_version}), stamp: ${z_stamp}"
  else
    # Refresh: derive locked version from the existing envelope and compute the
    # present-set (enveloped + orphan members) to pass as _RBGL_PODVM_PRESERVED.
    buc_step "Computing present-set for refresh (reading existing :rbi_vouch envelope)"
    local -r z_vouch_dir="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}refresh_vouch"
    rm -rf "${z_vouch_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to clear refresh vouch scratch dir"
    local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
    zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact "${z_token}" "${z_pkg}" "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" "${z_vouch_dir}" \
      || buc_die "No :${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH} at ${z_pkg} — touchmark not present or not yet vouched"
    local -r z_vouch_json="${z_vouch_dir}/vouch.json"
    test -f "${z_vouch_json}" || buc_die "vouch.json missing in :${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH} artifact for ${z_stamp}"

    # Derive the locked version from any member's rblv_origin (format "<family>:<version>").
    z_version=$(jq -r '(.rblv_members // [])[0].rblv_origin | split(":")[1] // empty' "${z_vouch_json}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to derive version from existing envelope for ${z_stamp}"
    test -n "${z_version}" || buc_die "Existing envelope carries no rblv_origin in rblv_members — cannot derive version"
    buc_info "Refresh locked to version: ${z_version} (from existing envelope)"
    buc_info "Refresh stamp (reused): ${z_stamp}"

    # Compute the present-set: members already in the existing envelope (preserve verbatim)
    # plus any orphan tags in GAR (tag present but absent from envelope → recover).
    # SOURCE OF TRUTH = the GAR package's member tags (never the envelope alone).
    # Architecture H: the full present-set is passed as ONE substitution to the select step.
    # Ceiling note: worst case 8 members ≈ 2.7 KB < 4000-byte substitution limit (CBh_103).
    # A future widening past ~11 members must fall to in-pool recovery instead.
    local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg//\//%2F}/tags?pageSize=1000"
    local -r z_tags_infix="rbld_immure_refresh_tags"
    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
    rbuh_require_ok "List tags for ${z_stamp}" "${z_tags_infix}"
    local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"

    # Build preserved-member JSON array: enveloped members get verbatim rblv_members[] entry;
    # orphan tags (in GAR, absent from envelope) get a recovered entry with rblv_verification
    # "recorded" and rblv_acquired_at from the GAR tag createTime.
    local -r z_preserved_file="${ZRBLD_IMMURE_PREFIX}preserved.json"
    jq -r \
      --arg sprue "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
      --arg vouch "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
      --arg family "${z_quay_family}" \
      --arg version "${z_version}" \
      --argjson existing_members "$(jq '.rblv_members // []' "${z_vouch_json}")" \
      '
        # All non-vouch member tags present in GAR
        ( [.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "") | select(. != $vouch and startswith($sprue))] ) as $gar_tags |
        # Index existing envelope members by rblv_name
        ( $existing_members | map({(.rblv_name): .}) | add // {} ) as $env_idx |
        # For each GAR tag: enveloped -> preserve verbatim; orphan -> recover
        [ $gar_tags[] |
          if $env_idx[.] then
            $env_idx[.]
          else
            {
              "rblv_name": .,
              "rblv_origin": ($family + ":" + $version),
              "rblv_digest": "(recovered-orphan)",
              "rblv_verification": "recorded",
              "rblv_tags": [.],
              "rblv_acquired_at": "unknown",
              "rblv_capture_build": "unknown"
            }
          end
        ]
      ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_preserved_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to compute present-set for refresh of ${z_stamp}"

    z_preserved_members=$(jq -c '.' "${z_preserved_file}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to compact preserved-member JSON"
    local z_pcount=""
    z_pcount=$(jq 'length' "${z_preserved_file}") \
      || buc_die "Failed to count preserved members"
    buc_info "Present-set computed: ${z_pcount} existing members to preserve/recover"
  fi

  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_immure_submit "${z_token}" "${z_brand}" "${z_quay_family}" "${z_version}" \
    "${z_selection}" "${z_stamp}" "${z_preserved_members}"
  zrbld_immure_extract "${z_brand}"

  buc_success "Immure (${z_mode}) complete: ${z_quay_family}:${z_version} -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}
